options: it is continuing to thin at an alarming rate.
It has already returned to its pre-industrial levels.
It is showing signs of healing and is expected to recover.
It has been completely replaced by a new, man-made shield.
questions.explanation: Meskipun ada tantangan (gas rumah kaca), teks menyimpulkan bahwa ada tanda-tanda pemulihan dan diperkirakan lapisan ozon dapat kembali ke tingkat tahun 1980 di pertengahan abad ke-21.

questions.questionText: What is a potential 'new' problem mentioned in connection with CFC replacements?
options: They are even more destructive to the ozone layer than CFCs.
They are potent greenhouse gases that contribute to climate change.
They make refrigeration systems much less efficient.
They are too expensive for developing nations to afford.
questions.explanation: Masalah baru yang muncul adalah bahwa beberapa zat pengganti CFC, meskipun aman untuk ozon, merupakan gas rumah kaca yang kuat dan berkontribusi pada pemanasan global.

questions.questionText: What can be inferred about the future of environmental protection from this passage?
options: Technology will always cause more harm than good.
International cooperation is a key factor in solving global threats.
The ozone layer is no longer a concern for scientists.
Political treaties are usually ineffective at changing human behavior.
questions.explanation: Karena krisis ini memerlukan respons global (Protokol Montreal), dapat disimpulkan bahwa kerja sama internasional adalah faktor kunci yang diperlukan untuk mengatasi ancaman lingkungan global.

passages.title: The Harlem Renaissance: A Cultural Rebirth
passages.content:
The Harlem Renaissance was an unprecedented explosion of intellectual, social, and artistic expression that took place in Harlem, New York City, spanning roughly from the end of World War I through the mid-1930s. At the time, it was known as the "New Negro Movement," named after the 1925 anthology by Alain Locke. This period marked a profound turning point in African American history, as it was the first time that mainstream publishers and critics began to take African American literature, music, theater, and visual arts seriously. The movement was not merely a local phenomenon but a central component of a larger "Great Migration," as hundreds of thousands of African Americans fled the oppressive Jim Crow laws of the South in search of better economic opportunities and social freedoms in Northern industrial cities.

In the realm of literature, the Harlem Renaissance produced a generation of writers who sought to challenge racial stereotypes and express a new sense of pride in their heritage. Figures like Langston Hughes and Zora Neale Hurston became icons of this era. Hughes’s poetry often incorporated the rhythms of jazz and blues, reflecting the daily lives and struggles of ordinary black people. Hurston, an anthropologist as well as a novelist, used her work to explore the rich folklore and oral traditions of the rural South, most notably in her masterpiece, *Their Eyes Were Watching God*. Meanwhile, writers like Claude McKay used militant verse to protest against racial injustice and violence, demonstrating the political dimensions of the cultural rebirth.

Music, particularly jazz and blues, became the definitive soundtrack of the Harlem Renaissance. The migration of musicians from New Orleans and Chicago brought new sounds to New York, leading to the creation of legendary venues like the Cotton Club and the Apollo Theater. Legends such as Duke Ellington and Louis Armstrong revolutionized American music, blending complex orchestration with improvisational brilliance. For many white Americans, visiting Harlem’s jazz clubs became a popular pastime, which helped to break down some social barriers even as segregation remained a harsh reality. The Cotton Club, while famous for launching the careers of many black performers, often maintained a strict "whites-only" policy for its audience, highlighting the deep-seated ironies of the era. This complex social dynamic forced many artists to balance their creative integrity with the demands of a segregated commercial market, yet they nevertheless succeeded in creating a globally influential musical legacy. The visual arts also flourished, with artists like Aaron Douglas using bold, geometric styles—later termed "African-style modernism"—to depict both the historical struggles and the future aspirations of the African American community.

The legacy of the Harlem Renaissance is immense, as it helped to lay the intellectual and artistic foundations for the Civil Rights Movement of the 1950s and 60s. It gave African Americans a new sense of identity, self-determination, and cultural authority that could not be easily ignored. Although the onset of the Great Depression in 1929 slowed the momentum of the movement and decimated the economic support for many artists, its impact survived. The Harlem Renaissance proved that African American culture was a vital and indispensable part of the American experience, forever changing the way the nation understood race, art, and identity.

questions.questionText: What was the primary driver of the Harlem Renaissance mentioned in the passage?
options: A sudden increase in government funding for the arts.
The mass migration of African Americans from the rural South to Northern cities.
The end of segregation in New York City’s public spaces.
A decline in the popularity of European-style literature.
questions.explanation: Penyebab utama gerakan Harlem Renaissance adalah 'Great Migration', yaitu perpindahan ratusan ribu orang Afrika-Amerika dari wilayah Selatan yang represif (Jim Crow) ke kota-kota industri di Utara untuk mencari peluang ekonomi dan kebebasan sosial.

questions.questionText: The word "unprecedented" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Unexpected
Never seen before
Politically motivated
Highly controversial
questions.explanation: "Unprecedented" berarti belum pernah terjadi sebelumnya atau tidak tertandingi. Ini menunjukkan bahwa gerakan tersebut merupakan peristiwa yang sangat unik dan luar biasa pada masanya.

questions.questionText: According to the passage, how did Langston Hughes’s poetry differ from earlier styles?
options: It focused exclusively on elite members of society.
It was written in traditional European xonnet forms.
It incorporated the musical rhythms of jazz and blues.
It avoided mentioning the struggles of daily life.
questions.explanation: Puisi Langston Hughes berbeda karena ia sering memasukkan ritme musik jazz dan blues ke dalam karyanya, yang berfungsi untuk mencerminkan kehidupan sehari-hari dan perjuangan masyarakat kulit hitam.

questions.questionText: Why does the author mention Zora Neale Hurston's background in anthropology?
options: To explain why she eventually stopped writing novels.
To highlight her focus on folklore and oral traditions.
To suggest that her work was more scientific than artistic.
To show that she was the only educated writer of the era.
questions.explanation: Latar belakang Hurston sebagai antropolog disebutkan untuk menjelaskan mengapa ia berfokus pada eksplorasi dan penggalian cerita rakyat serta tradisi lisan di wilayah Selatan.

questions.questionText: What paradox is suggested regarding the popularity of jazz clubs in Harlem?
options: The clubs were popular with white audiences even though segregation still existed.
Most jazz musicians actually preferred to play classical music.
The clubs were only successful during the Great Depression.
Musicians were famous in Harlem but unknown in other parts of New York.
questions.explanation: Paradoksnya adalah bahwa meskipun kunjungan ke klub jazz populer di kalangan warga kulit putih, segregasi rasial masih merupakan kenyataan yang keras pada saat itu. Ini menunjukkan kontradiksi antara budaya dan realitas sosial.

questions.questionText: The phrase "African-style modernism" refers to the work of which individual?
options: Alain Locke
Duke Ellington
Aaron Douglas
Claude McKay
questions.explanation: Aaron Douglas adalah seniman yang menggunakan gaya visual geometris yang berani dalam karyanya, yang kemudian disebut sebagai 'African-style modernism'.

questions.questionText: In paragraph 4, the word "decimated" is closest in meaning to:
options: Improved
Severely damaged
Ignored
Organized
questions.explanation: "Decimated" berarti sangat merusak atau menghancurkan. Dalam konteks ini, kata tersebut menunjukkan bahwa Depresi Besar (Great Depression) sangat merusak dan mengurangi dukungan ekonomi bagi banyak seniman.

questions.questionText: What long-term impact did the Harlem Renaissance have, according to the text?
options: It led to the immediate end of all racial discrimination.
It created the economic conditions for the Great Depression.
It provided the intellectual and artistic basis for the Civil Rights Movement.
It caused African Americans to return to the rural South.
questions.explanation: Dampak jangka panjang gerakan ini adalah bahwa ia meletakkan dasar intelektual dan artistik yang penting bagi Gerakan Hak Sipil yang terjadi pada tahun 1950-an dan 60-an.

questions.questionText: Which of the following was NOT mentioned as an area of expression during the movement?
options: Literature
Film Production
Visual Arts
Theater
questions.explanation: Teks secara eksplisit menyebutkan sastra (literature), musik, teater, dan seni rupa. Produksi film (Film Production) tidak disebutkan sebagai area ekspresi utama gerakan tersebut.

questions.questionText: The author’s attitude toward the Harlem Renaissance can best be described as:
options: Critical of its lack of political focus.
Appreciative of its cultural significance.
Neutral regarding its historical impact.
Skeptical of its lasting influence.
questions.explanation: Penulis menunjukkan sikap apresiatif (menghargai) terhadap gerakan ini dengan menyebutnya sebagai bagian yang 'vital and indispensable' (penting dan sangat diperlukan), serta mengakui warisannya.

passages.title: Bioluminescence: Nature’s Living Light
passages.content:
Bioluminescence is the remarkable ability of living organisms to produce and emit light through an internal chemical reaction. This phenomenon, while rare on land, is incredibly widespread in the world's oceans, where it is estimated that nearly 70 to 90 percent of deep-sea life possesses some form of light-producing capability. Bioluminescence is a form of chemiluminescence—the production of light from a chemical reaction—specifically one that occurs within a biological system. Unlike the light produced by a typical incandescent bulb, which generates significant heat, bioluminescence is often referred to as "cold light." In these biological reactions, less than 20 percent of the energy is lost as thermal radiation, making it one of the most efficient light-producing mechanisms in nature.

The chemistry behind this living light involves two primary components: a light-emitting molecule called luciferin and an enzyme called luciferase. When the luciferin reacts with oxygen, the reaction is accelerated by the luciferase enzyme, resulting in the release of photons. The specific color of the light—ranging from the common blue-green of the deep ocean to the rare red light produced by certain predatory fish—is determined by the structural arrangement of the luciferin molecules. Some organisms produce their own chemicals, while others, like the Hawaiian bobtail squid, host colonies of bioluminescent bacteria in specialized "light organs." This symbiotic relationship allows the squid to use the bacteria's light for its own survival while providing the bacteria with a steady supply of nutrients. In addition to these primary components, some organisms require a trigger, such as a physical disturbance or a chemical signal, to initiate the reaction, adding another layer of complexity to this biological torch.

Organisms have evolved bioluminescence for a diverse array of survival strategies. Many use light for communication; fireflies, for instance, use rhythmic flashes to attract potential mates in the dark. In the deep sea, where sunlight never penetrates, light is used as a tool for both hunting and defense. The anglerfish uses a glowing lure suspended from its head to entice curious prey into its massive jaws. Conversely, many species use bioluminescence for camouflage through a process known as counter-illumination. By emitting light from their undersides that matches the intensity of the faint sunlight filtering down from above, these animals can mask their silhouettes from predators lurking below. Other organisms, such as certain jellyfish and squids, release clouds of glowing fluid to startle and distract attackers, allowing the creature to escape into the darkness. Some deep-sea shrimp even "spew" light to blind their predators, a spectacular defense mechanism that momentarily dazzles anything in its path.

Scientific interest in bioluminescence extends far beyond mere curiosity. The genes responsible for producing luciferase have become invaluable tools in modern biotechnology and medical research. By attaching these "reporter genes" to specific strands of DNA, scientists can track the spread of diseases, monitor the effectiveness of new drugs, and visualize the inner workings of living cells in real-time. Furthermore, researchers are exploring the potential of using bioluminescent trees or plants as sustainable, energy-free street lighting. Imagine a city where the parks are illuminated not by electric lamps, but by the soft, natural glow of genetically enhanced vegetation. Such an innovation could drastically reduce urban energy consumption and light pollution. The study of nature’s living light continues to illuminate new possibilities in fields ranging from environmental monitoring to advanced cancer therapies, proving that the secrets of the deep may hold the key to future technological breakthroughs.

questions.questionText: What is the defining characteristic of "cold light," as described in the passage?
options: It is only produced by organisms living in freezing water.
It requires no oxygen to be produced.
Most of the energy is converted into light rather than heat.
It is invisible to the human eye without special equipment.
questions.explanation: "Cold light" (cahaya dingin) merujuk pada sifatnya yang sangat efisien: kurang dari 20 persen energi dalam reaksi bioluminesensi hilang sebagai radiasi termal (panas), berbeda dengan lampu pijar biasa yang menghasilkan banyak panas.

questions.questionText: The word "widespread" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Limited
Common
Dangerous
Expensive
questions.explanation: "Widespread" berarti tersebar luas atau umum terjadi. Dalam konteks ini, bioluminesensi sangat meluas di seluruh lautan.

questions.questionText: According to paragraph 2, what determines the specific color of the bioluminescent light?
options: The depth of the water where the organism lives.
The temperature of the surrounding environment.
The structural arrangement of the luciferin molecules.
The amount of oxygen available for the reaction.
questions.explanation: Warna cahaya yang dihasilkan ditentukan oleh susunan struktural molekul lusiferin (luciferin) yang terlibat dalam reaksi kimia tersebut.

questions.questionText: Which of the following best describes the relationship between the Hawaiian bobtail squid and bioluminescent bacteria?
options: A competitive relationship where both fight for resources.
A parasitic relationship where the squid is harmed.
A symbiotic relationship where both benefits.
A predatory relationship where the squid eats the bacteria.
questions.explanation: Ini adalah hubungan simbiosis. Cumi-cumi menggunakan cahaya bakteri untuk bertahan hidup (manfaat bagi cumi-cumi), sementara bakteri mendapatkan pasokan nutrisi yang stabil.

questions.questionText: How does counter-illumination help an organism survive?
options: It allows the animal to see better in the dark.
It makes the animal look much larger to predators.
It hides the animal’s silhouette from predators below.
It attracts smaller fish that the animal can eat.
questions.explanation: Counter-illumination adalah mekanisme kamuflase di mana hewan memancarkan cahaya dari bagian bawah tubuhnya yang sesuai dengan intensitas cahaya redup dari atas, sehingga menyamarkan siluet mereka dari predator yang berada di bawah.

questions.questionText: The word "entice" in paragraph 3 is closest in meaning to:
options: Scare
Capture
Lure
Warn
questions.explanation: "Entice" berarti membujuk atau memikat. Dalam konteks ini, cahaya yang dipancarkan oleh ikan anglerfish berfungsi sebagai umpan (lure) untuk memancing mangsa.

questions.questionText: According to the passage, how is bioluminescence used in modern medicine?
options: As a way to sterilize surgical instruments.
To provide light during complicated surgeries.
To track the spread of diseases within the body.
As a supplement to increase human energy levels.
questions.explanation: Dalam penelitian modern, gen yang bertanggung jawab untuk menghasilkan luciferase digunakan sebagai 'reporter genes' untuk melacak penyebaran penyakit dan memantau efektivitas obat-obatan.

questions.questionText: In paragraph 4, the author mentions "bioluminescent trees" as an example of:
options: A natural disaster caused by genetic engineering.
A way to reduce energy consumption in cities.
An endangered species that needs protection.
The primary source of oxygen in urban areas.
questions.explanation: Pohon bioluminescent dieksplorasi sebagai inovasi potensial untuk pencahayaan jalan yang berkelanjutan dan bebas energi, sehingga dapat mengurangi konsumsi energi perkotaan.

questions.questionText: What is the author’s primary purpose in writing this passage?
options: To explain the chemical and biological importance of bioluminescence.
To warn readers about the dangers of deep-sea exploration.
To compare bioluminescence with traditional electricity.
To argue that all deep-sea animals should be kept in aquariums.
questions.explanation: Tujuan utama penulis adalah untuk menjelaskan secara rinci mekanisme kimia dan biologi di balik bioluminesensi, fungsinya dalam kelangsungan hidup organisme, dan potensi aplikasinya di bidang modern.

questions.questionText: It can be inferred from the passage that bioluminescence is:
options: More common in terrestrial environments than in marine ones.
A relatively new development in evolutionary history.
An area of study with significant future potential.
Only possible for organisms that do not require oxygen.
questions.explanation: Meskipun bioluminesensi sudah ada, bagian akhir teks menyoroti bahwa penelitiannya terus menerus membuka kemungkinan teknologi baru di masa depan, menunjukkan potensi besar bidang ini.

passages.title: The Wright Brothers: Mastering the Skies
passages.content:
Orville and Wilbur Wright, two self-taught engineers from Dayton, Ohio, are celebrated as the pioneers who invented, built, and flew the world’s first successful motor-operated airplane. Their journey from running a bicycle repair and sales shop to achieving controlled, powered flight is a testament to the power of systematic experimentation and perseverance. Unlike many of their contemporaries who were well-funded and focused on building powerful engines, the Wright brothers realized that the true secret to flight lay not in raw power, but in aerodynamic control. They spent years studying the work of previous aviation experimenters and observing the flight patterns of birds, which led them to believe that a pilot must be able to control an aircraft in three dimensions. This shift in focus from engine power to pilot control was perhaps their greatest intellectual contribution to the field of aeronautics.

The brothers identified three fundamental challenges that had to be overcome: lift, propulsion, and control. To solve the problem of lift, they built a small wind tunnel in their workshop to test the performance of hundreds of different wing shapes. This allowed them to collect accurate data on how various curves affected the air pressure above and below the wing, replacing the unreliable formulas of the past. For propulsion, they were unable to find a suitable engine that was both powerful and lightweight, so they designed and built their own four-cylinder internal combustion engine with the help of their shop mechanic, Charlie Taylor. However, their most revolutionary breakthrough was in control. They developed a system called "wing-warping," which allowed the pilot to twist the edges of the wings to bank the aircraft and maintain balance, a concept that evolved into the modern aileron used on almost every aircraft today.

On December 17, 1903, at the wind-swept dunes of Kitty Hawk, North Carolina, the brothers were ready to test their "Flyer." Orville took the controls for the first historic flight, which lasted a mere 12 seconds and covered 120 feet. While seemingly modest, it was the first time a manned machine had taken off under its own power and landed at a point as high as where it had started. Throughout that day, they took turns flying, with the longest flight performed by Wilbur, who stayed aloft for 59 seconds and covered 852 feet. Despite this monumental achievement, news of their success was initially met with widespread skepticism, and it would be several years before the significance of their invention was fully recognized by the public and the military. The brothers continued to refine their designs in private, eventually demonstrating their Flyer III, the world's first truly practical airplane, in 1905.

The impact of the Wright brothers’ invention cannot be overstated. It effectively shrank the world, transforming transportation and making global connectivity a reality within a few decades. The principles of flight they discovered—specifically the need for three-axis control (pitch, roll, and yaw)—remain the foundation of modern aviation to this day. Beyond transportation, the airplane fundamentally changed the nature of warfare and exploration, allowing humans to reach the most remote corners of the planet. Orville and Wilbur Wright did not just build a machine; they launched an era that would eventually see humanity reach the stars. Their legacy lives on in every commercial flight and in the exploration of other worlds, proving that innovation often comes from those who are willing to look at old problems from a completely new perspective and work tirelessly to prove their vision.

questions.questionText: According to the passage, what did the Wright brothers believe was the most important factor for successful flight?
options: Having the most powerful engine available.
Receiving significant financial backing from the government.
Mastering the ability to control the aircraft.
Using the heaviest materials to ensure stability.
questions.explanation: Wright bersaudara menyadari bahwa rahasia sejati penerbangan bukan terletak pada kekuatan mesin mentah (raw power), melainkan pada kontrol aerodinamis dan kemampuan pilot untuk mengendalikan pesawat dalam tiga dimensi.

questions.questionText: The word "contemporaries" in paragraph 1 refers to:
options: People living at the same time.
Scientific predecessors from the past.
Family members who supported the brothers.
Famous pilots from the modern era.
questions.explanation: "Contemporaries" mengacu pada orang-orang yang hidup dan beroperasi pada periode waktu yang sama dengan Orville dan Wilbur Wright (sezaman).

questions.questionText: Why did the Wright brothers build a wind tunnel?
options: To test the durability of their bicycle tires.
To gather data on the performance of different wing shapes.
To generate electricity for their workshop in Dayton.
To demonstrate the power of their new engine.
questions.explanation: Terowongan angin dibangun untuk menguji kinerja ratusan bentuk sayap yang berbeda dan mengumpulkan data akurat tentang bagaimana berbagai kurva sayap memengaruhi tekanan udara di atas dan di bawah sayap (untuk mengatasi masalah gaya angkat/lift).

questions.questionText: How did the Wright brothers solve the problem of propulsion?
options: They bought a high-powered engine from a car manufacturer.
They used a steam-powered turbine.
They designed and built their own lightweight engine.
They relied on strong winds at Kitty Hawk to push the plane.
questions.explanation: Karena mereka tidak dapat menemukan mesin yang cukup bertenaga dan ringan, Wright bersaudara merancang dan membangun sendiri mesin pembakaran internal empat silinder mereka.

questions.questionText: The system of "wing-warping" was revolutionary because it allowed the pilot to:
options: Increase the speed of the engine.
Balance and bank the aircraft by twisting wing edges.
Communicate with people on the ground.
Land the plane safely on water.
questions.explanation: 'Wing-warping' adalah terobosan revolusioner karena memungkinkan pilot memutar tepi sayap untuk memiringkan pesawat (banking) dan mempertahankan keseimbangan.

questions.questionText: The word "modest" in paragraph 3 is used to describe:
options: The character of Orville Wright.
The height of the dunes at Kitty Hawk.
The duration and distance of the first flight.
The cost of building the Flyer.
questions.explanation: "Modest" (sederhana/kecil) di sini digunakan untuk menggambarkan durasi penerbangan pertama yang singkat (12 detik) dan jaraknya (120 kaki), meskipun peristiwa itu sangat bersejarah.

questions.questionText: What was unusual about the takeoff of the first historic flight?
options: It was launched from a high mountain.
It took off under its own power.
It was piloted by two people at once.
It was done without any wings attached.
questions.explanation: Hal yang tidak biasa adalah bahwa penerbangan bersejarah pertama ini adalah kali pertama sebuah mesin berawak lepas landas dengan tenaganya sendiri.

questions.questionText: According to the passage, how did the public initially react to the flight?
options: With immediate enthusiasm and celebration.
With widespread doubt and skepticism.
By offering to buy the invention for the military.
By ignoring the event completely.
questions.explanation: Berita tentang kesuksesan mereka awalnya disambut dengan skeptisisme luas (widespread skepticism), dan butuh waktu bertahun-tahun sebelum signifikansi penemuan mereka diakui oleh publik.

questions.questionText: What fundamental principles of flight discovered by the Wrights are mentioned?
options: Speed, altitude, and temperature.
Pitch, roll, and yaw.
Gravity, friction, and tension.
Steam, coal, and oil.
questions.explanation: Prinsip-prinsip kunci yang mereka temukan adalah kebutuhan akan kontrol tiga poros (pitch, roll, dan yaw), yang kini menjadi dasar dari semua penerbangan modern.

questions.questionText: The phrase "shrank the world" in paragraph 4 suggests that the airplane:
options: Made the physical size of the Earth smaller.
Reduced the time needed to travel long distances.
Caused cities to become more crowded.
Was initially very small in size.
questions.explanation: Ungkapan 'shrank the world' menyiratkan bahwa penemuan pesawat terbang secara dramatis mengurangi waktu yang dibutuhkan untuk melakukan perjalanan jarak jauh, sehingga membuat konektivitas global menjadi kenyataan.

passages.title: The Great Barrier Reef: A Fragile Marine Masterpiece
passages.content:
The Great Barrier Reef, extending over 2,300 kilometers along the northeastern coast of Australia, is the world's most extensive coral reef ecosystem. This natural wonder is so massive that it can be seen from outer space, yet it is composed of billions of tiny organisms known as coral polyps. These polyps secrete calcium carbonate to form the hard skeletons that serve as the building blocks of the reef. Far from being a single, continuous structure, the Great Barrier Reef is a complex mosaic of over 2,900 individual reefs and approximately 900 islands, ranging from small sandy cays to large, vegetated landmasses. The sheer scale and biological complexity of the system led to its designation as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1981, recognizing its "outstanding universal value" to humanity.

The biodiversity within the reef is staggering, supporting a vast array of marine life that depends on the coral structures for food and shelter. It is home to more than 1,500 species of fish, ranging from tiny, vibrant damselfish to massive whale sharks. In addition to fish, the reef supports thirty species of whales, dolphins, and porpoises, as well as six of the world's seven species of marine turtles. The coral themselves are incredibly diverse, with over 400 types of hard and soft corals providing the foundation for this "underwater rainforest." This intricate web of life is not just a biological marvel but also a vital economic resource for Australia, contributing billions of dollars annually to the national economy through tourism and sustainable fishing industries.

However, the Great Barrier Reef is currently facing unprecedented challenges that threaten its very existence. The most significant of these is climate change, specifically the rise in ocean temperatures. When water temperatures remain too high for extended periods, corals undergo a process called bleaching. In this stressed state, the corals expel the symbiotic algae—zooxanthellae—that live within their tissues and provide them with both their vibrant colors and essential nutrients. Without these algae, the corals turn white and, if the heat persists, eventually die. Since 2016, the reef has experienced several massive bleaching events that have significantly reduced coral cover in many regions. Furthermore, ocean acidification, caused by the absorption of excess carbon dioxide from the atmosphere, hinders the ability of corals to build their skeletons, further weakening the reef's structure.

In addition to global climate issues, local threats such as poor water quality and the outbreak of crown-of-thorns starfish continue to plague the ecosystem. Runoff from agricultural land can introduce excess nutrients and sediments into the reef waters, which promotes the growth of harmful algae and can lead to crown-of-thorns starfish outbreaks. These starfish are voracious predators of coral, and in large numbers, they can devastate entire reef sections within weeks. Despite these daunting challenges, extensive conservation efforts are underway. Scientists are exploring innovative solutions such as "coral gardening," where resilient coral strains are grown in nurseries and then transplanted onto damaged reefs. There is also a major focus on improving water quality and implementing stricter regulations on fishing and tourism. The fate of the Great Barrier Reef remains a critical barometer for the health of the world's oceans, reminding us that even the most robust natural systems are vulnerable to human-induced environmental changes.

questions.questionText: What is the primary role of coral polyps according to the passage?
options: They act as predators that control the fish population.
They secrete the material that forms the physical structure of the reef.
They migrate across the ocean to find new islands to inhabit.
They provide oxygen directly to the atmosphere through photosynthesis.
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'They secrete the material that forms the physical structure of the reef'. Teks secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa polip karang mengeluarkan kalsium karbonat untuk membentuk kerangka keras yang menjadi blok bangunan terumbu karang. Pilihan lain salah karena tidak sesuai dengan fungsi utama polip.

questions.questionText: The word "mosaic" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Single unit
Patterned arrangement
Solid wall
Hidden treasure
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'Patterned arrangement'. Dalam konteks ini, 'mosaic' merujuk pada susunan terfragmentasi namun terintegrasi dari banyak bagian (lebih dari 2.900 terumbu karang individu dan 900 pulau). Pilihan lain tidak mencerminkan ide tentang susunan beragam bagian.

questions.questionText: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a type of animal supported by the reef?
options: Marine turtles
Whales and dolphins
Terrestrial birds
Over 1,500 species of fish
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'Terrestrial birds'. Teks menyebutkan ikan, lumba-lumba, paus, dan kura-kura laut. Tidak ada penyebutan spesifik mengenai burung darat (terrestrial birds) sebagai bagian dari ekosistem terumbu karang ini.

questions.questionText: What triggers the process of coral bleaching?
options: An increase in the population of crown-of-thorns starfish.
The presence of too much calcium carbonate in the water.
Prolonged exposure to high water temperatures.
The over-harvesting of colorful fish by the tourism industry.
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'Prolonged exposure to high water temperatures'. Teks menjelaskan bahwa pemutihan karang (bleaching) terjadi ketika suhu air tetap terlalu tinggi dalam jangka waktu lama, yang menyebabkan karang mengusir alga simbiosisnya.

questions.questionText: Why are zooxanthellae important to corals?
options: They protect the corals from predatory starfish.
They provide corals with nutrients and their color.
They help corals move to cooler parts of the ocean.
They convert carbon dioxide into calcium carbonate.
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'They provide corals with nutrients and their color'. Alga zooxanthellae hidup di dalam jaringan karang dan menyediakan nutrisi penting serta warna-warna cerah bagi karang.

questions.questionText: According to paragraph 4, what is "coral gardening"?
options: A method of removing harmful algae from the seabed.
Growing resilient corals in nurseries to transplant them later.
A type of sustainable fishing practice.
Educating tourists on how to visit the reef safely.
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'Growing resilient corals in nurseries to transplant them later'. Coral gardening adalah solusi inovatif di mana strain karang yang tahan terhadap perubahan ditumbuhkan dalam pembibitan (nurseries) sebelum dipindahkan ke terumbu yang rusak.

questions.questionText: The word "voracious" in paragraph 4 is closest in meaning to:
options: Slow-moving
Extremely hungry
Very rare
Harmless
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'Extremely hungry'. Dalam konteks ini, 'voracious' berarti sangat lapar atau rakus. Ini merujuk pada bintang laut mahkota duri yang memakan karang dalam jumlah besar.

questions.questionText: What can be inferred about the economic impact of the Great Barrier Reef?
options: It is primarily a cost to the Australian government due to conservation expenses.
Its value is mostly historical rather than financial.
It plays a significant role in Australia's national economy through various industries.
It will likely be replaced by industrial fishing in the near future.
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'It plays a significant role in Australia's national economy through various industries'. Teks menyatakan bahwa terumbu karang memberikan kontribusi miliaran dolar setiap tahun melalui pariwisata dan industri perikanan berkelanjutan.

questions.questionText: The author mentions the "crown-of-thorns starfish" in order to illustrate:
options: A successful conservation story.
A local threat that can devastate reef sections.
A beautiful creature that attracts divers.
A symbiotic partner of the coral polyps.
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'A local threat that can devastate reef sections'. Bintang laut mahkota duri disebutkan sebagai ancaman lokal (local threat) yang merupakan predator rakus dan mampu menghancurkan bagian terumbu karang dalam hitungan minggu.

questions.questionText: What is the author's overall tone toward the future of the Great Barrier Reef?
options: Completely indifferent
Highly optimistic and celebratory
Concerned yet focused on potential solutions
Dismissive of the environmental warnings
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'Concerned yet focused on potential solutions'. Penulis mengungkapkan kekhawatiran serius tentang ancaman (climate change, bleaching) tetapi juga menyoroti upaya konservasi dan solusi inovatif seperti coral gardening, menunjukkan sikap yang peduli namun realistis.

passages.title: Marie Curie: A Legacy of Scientific Brilliance
passages.content:
Marie Skłodowska Curie, born in Warsaw, Poland, in 1867, remains one of the most celebrated and influential figures in the history of science. Her journey to worldwide recognition was marked by extraordinary intellect, unrelenting perseverance, and a deep-seated passion for discovery that transcended the social and political barriers of her time. In an era when women were largely excluded from higher education and scientific research, Curie moved to Paris in 1891 to study at the Sorbonne. There, she lived in poverty while pursuing her degrees in physics and mathematics, eventually becoming the first woman to hold a professorship at the university. Her life’s work would not only redefine the field of physics but also provide the foundation for some of the most critical advances in modern medicine.

Curie’s most significant scientific breakthrough came through her research on radioactivity, a term she herself coined. Working alongside her husband, Pierre Curie, she sought to identify the source of the mysterious rays emitted by uranium salts, which had been discovered by Henri Becquerel. In their cramped and poorly equipped laboratory, the Curies painstakingly processed tons of pitchblende, a uranium-rich mineral. This arduous labor led to the discovery of two previously unknown elements: polonium, named after Marie’s native Poland, and radium. These discoveries were so monumental that in 1903, Marie and Pierre Curie, along with Becquerel, were awarded the Nobel Prize in Physics. This achievement made Marie the first woman ever to receive a Nobel Prize, shattering a significant glass ceiling in the academic world.

Despite the tragic death of Pierre in 1906, Marie continued her research with even greater intensity. Her commitment to science remained unshaken, and in 1911, she was awarded a second Nobel Prize, this time in Chemistry, for her work in isolating pure radium and studying the properties of radioactive elements. To this day, she remains the only person, regardless of gender, to have won Nobel Prizes in two different scientific fields. Curie's refusal to patent her discovery of the radium isolation process, despite its potential to bring her immense wealth, reflected her belief that scientific knowledge should be used for the benefit of all humanity. She viewed her work as a public service, a perspective that would soon prove vital during one of the darkest periods of European history.

During World War I, Curie recognized the immediate clinical applications of her research. She realized that X-ray technology could be used to locate bullets and shrapnel in wounded soldiers, potentially saving thousands of lives on the front lines. She developed mobile radiography units, which became affectionately known as "Petites Curies," and personally drove them to field hospitals. Along with her daughter Irène, she trained hundreds of nurses and doctors in the use of these units. This humanitarian effort demonstrated that pure scientific research could have immediate, life-saving practical applications. Tragically, her prolonged exposure to radiation during her years of research eventually took a toll on her health, leading to her death from aplastic anemia in 1934. Marie Curie’s legacy persists not only in the periodic table but also in the countless lives saved by radiation therapy for cancer, proving that a single individual’s dedication can change the course of human history.

questions.questionText: What was the primary obstacle Marie Curie faced in her early academic career?
options: A lack of interest in the subject of physics.
Social and political barriers that restricted women's education.
The refusal of the Sorbonne to admit foreign students.
Her inability to master the French language.
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'Social and political barriers that restricted women's education'. Teks menjelaskan bahwa pada era tersebut, perempuan sebagian besar dikecualikan dari pendidikan tinggi dan penelitian ilmiah, sehingga Curie harus menghadapi hambatan sosial dan politik untuk dapat belajar di Sorbonne.

questions.questionText: The word "arduous" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
options: Creative
Requiring great effort
Secretive
Highly profitable
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'Requiring great effort'. Kata 'arduous' berarti sangat sulit atau melelahkan. Ini merujuk pada kerja keras dan proses yang sangat sulit dalam memproses berton-ton pitchblende.

questions.questionText: Which discovery is Marie Curie credited with naming?
options: X-ray technology
Radioactivity
The Sorbonne
Pitchblende
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'Radioactivity'. Teks menyatakan bahwa Marie Curie sendiri yang menciptakan istilah 'radioactivity' (radioaktivitas), sebuah istilah yang dia gunakan untuk menjelaskan penelitiannya.

questions.questionText: According to the passage, why did Curie choose the name "polonium" for the new element?
options: It was the name of the laboratory where it was discovered.
It was a tribute to her native country, Poland.
It was named after a famous physicist of the era.
It was derived from the Latin word for "powerful."
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'It was a tribute to her native country, Poland'. Elemen polonium dinamai berdasarkan negara asal Marie Curie, yaitu Polandia.

questions.questionText: What is unique about Marie Curie’s Nobel Prize achievements?
options: She is the only person to win a Nobel Prize in two different scientific fields.
She won more Nobel Prizes than any other scientist in history.
She was the youngest person ever to receive the award.
She won her first prize without the help of any collaborators.
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'She is the only person to win a Nobel Prize in two different scientific fields'. Curie adalah satu-satunya orang yang memenangkan Hadiah Nobel dalam dua bidang ilmiah yang berbeda (Fisika dan Kimia). Pilihan lain tidak sesuai dengan fakta tersebut.

questions.questionText: The phrase "refusal to patent" in paragraph 3 suggests that Curie:
options: Did not understand the legal system.
Was worried that her work was not original enough.
Put the welfare of humanity above personal financial gain.
Expected the government to pay her a salary instead.
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'Put the welfare of humanity above personal financial gain'. Penolakan Curie untuk mematenkan proses isolasi radium mencerminkan keyakinannya bahwa pengetahuan ilmiah harus digunakan untuk kepentingan seluruh umat manusia (public service), bukan semata-mata demi keuntungan finansial pribadi.

questions.questionText: What role did Marie Curie play during World War I?
options: She served as a high-ranking military strategist.
She developed and distributed mobile X-ray units to help wounded soldiers.
She focused exclusively on her laboratory research in Paris.
She wrote influential books against the outbreak of the war.
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'She developed and distributed mobile X-ray units to help wounded soldiers'. Selama Perang Dunia I, Curie mengembangkan unit radiografi seluler ("Petites Curies") dan secara pribadi mengemudikannya ke rumah sakit lapangan untuk menemukan peluru dan serpihan di tentara yang terluka.

questions.questionText: The word "toll" in paragraph 4 is closest in meaning to:
options: Payment
Success
Negative impact
Sound
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'Negative impact'. Dalam konteks kesehatan, 'toll' merujuk pada dampak buruk atau kerusakan (kerugian) yang ditimbulkan oleh paparan radiasi yang berkepanjangan terhadap kesehatan Curie.

questions.questionText: What eventually led to Marie Curie’s death?
options: Injuries sustained during the war.
A tragic accident in her laboratory.
Long-term exposure to radioactive materials.
A viral infection that was common at the time.
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'Long-term exposure to radioactive materials'. Teks menyebutkan bahwa paparan radiasi yang berkepanjangan selama bertahun-tahun menyebabkan masalah kesehatan yang akhirnya berujung pada kematiannya karena anemia aplastik pada tahun 1934.

questions.questionText: Which of the following best summarizes the main idea of the passage?
options: The history of the Sorbonne during the late 19th century.
The collaborative nature of scientific discovery in the early 1900s.
The life and lasting impact of a pioneering female scientist.
The dangers of working with radioactive materials without protection.
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'The life and lasting impact of a pioneering female scientist'. Keseluruhan teks berfokus pada kehidupan Marie Curie, pencapaian ilmiahnya, dan warisan abadi yang ia tinggalkan dalam bidang fisika dan kedokteran.

passages.title: The Amazon Rainforest: The Lungs of a Changing Planet
passages.content:
The Amazon Rainforest, a vast expanse of tropical vegetation spanning over 5.5 million square kilometers, is the largest and most biodiverse rainforest on Earth. Often referred to as the "lungs of the planet," this immense ecosystem plays a critical role in regulating the global climate by absorbing vast amounts of carbon dioxide and producing approximately 20 percent of the world's oxygen. Stretching across nine South American nations—with the majority located in Brazil—the Amazon is sustained by the Amazon River, the world's largest river by volume of discharge. This interconnected web of rivers and forests creates a unique climate system that heavily influences rainfall patterns far beyond the borders of South America, affecting agriculture and water supplies across the Western Hemisphere.

The biological diversity found within the Amazon is nothing short of incredible. It is estimated to be home to nearly 10 percent of the world's known species, including approximately 40,000 plant species, 3,000 types of freshwater fish, and thousands of different birds, mammals, and insects. Within a single hectare of the Amazon, one might find more tree species than in the entire North American continent. This richness is not only a source of wonder but also a treasure trove for medical and scientific research. Many of the modern medicines used today, including treatments for malaria and certain types of cancer, have their origins in the unique chemical compounds discovered in Amazonian flora. For the hundreds of indigenous groups that have inhabited the forest for millennia, the Amazon is not just a resource but a sacred home that provides everything needed for survival.

Despite its global importance, the Amazon is currently under severe threat from human activities. Deforestation, primarily driven by large-scale agriculture, cattle ranching, and logging, has already resulted in the loss of nearly 20 percent of the forest's original canopy. As trees are cleared and burned, the carbon they had stored for centuries is released back into the atmosphere, contributing significantly to global greenhouse gas emissions. Furthermore, the loss of vegetation disrupts the local water cycle; trees in the Amazon release moisture through a process called transpiration, which creates the clouds and rain that sustain the forest itself. Scientists warn that the Amazon may be approaching a "tipping point," where the degradation becomes so severe that the rainforest can no longer sustain itself and eventually transforms into a dry savanna.

The consequences of losing the Amazon would be catastrophic for both local communities and the global environment. The loss of biodiversity would mean the permanent disappearance of species that could hold the key to future medical breakthroughs. Additionally, the disruption of rainfall patterns could devastate agriculture throughout the Americas, leading to food insecurity and economic instability. International efforts to protect the Amazon have gained momentum, with focus on sustainable development, stricter environmental regulations, and the recognition of indigenous land rights as a means of conservation. However, balancing economic growth with environmental preservation remains a complex challenge for the nations that share this vital resource. The future of the Amazon is inextricably linked to the future of the global climate, making its protection one of the most urgent environmental priorities of the 21st century.

questions.questionText: Why is the Amazon Rainforest often called the "lungs of the planet"?
options: It is the only place on Earth where people can breathe fresh air.
It produces a significant percentage of the world's oxygen.
It is shaped like a human lung when viewed from space.
It stores the majority of the world's freshwater underground.
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'It produces a significant percentage of the world's oxygen'. Amazon disebut 'paru-paru planet' karena fungsinya dalam mengatur iklim global dengan menyerap karbon dioksida dan menghasilkan sekitar 20% dari oksigen dunia.

questions.questionText: The word "discharge" in paragraph 1 refers to the:
options: Depth of the river at its deepest point.
Amount of water flowing out of the river.
Pollution released into the water by cities.
Fish that are caught in the river by locals.
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'Amount of water flowing out of the river'. Dalam konteks sungai, 'discharge' merujuk pada volume air total yang dialirkan atau dikeluarkan oleh sungai tersebut, yang dalam kasus Amazon adalah salah satu sungai terbesar berdasarkan volume.

questions.questionText: According to paragraph 1, how does the Amazon influence regions outside of South America?
options: By providing timber for global construction projects.
By influencing rainfall patterns and water supplies in the Western Hemisphere.
By attracting millions of tourists from North America every year.
By exporting its unique animal species to zoos around the world.
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'By influencing rainfall patterns and water supplies in the Western Hemisphere'. Teks menjelaskan bahwa sistem sungai dan hutan Amazon menciptakan sistem iklim unik yang sangat mempengaruhi pola curah hujan dan pasokan air di seluruh Belahan Bumi Barat.

questions.questionText: What is mentioned as a major benefit of Amazonian biodiversity for modern society?
options: It provides a source for many medical treatments.
it is the primary source of the world's paper supply.
It serves as a training ground for modern explorers.
It provides land for the world's largest cities.
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'It provides a source for many medical treatments'. Kekayaan hayati Amazon dianggap sebagai 'treasure trove' (harta karun) bagi penelitian medis, karena banyak obat modern berasal dari senyawa kimia unik di flora Amazon.

questions.questionText: The phrase "treasure trove" in paragraph 2 suggests that the Amazon is:
options: A place where ancient gold is hidden.
A valuable source of diverse and useful things.
A dangerous area that should be avoided by scientists.
A region that has already been fully explored.
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'A valuable source of diverse and useful things'. Istilah 'treasure trove' (harta karun) dalam konteks ini berarti sumber yang sangat berharga dan beragam dari berbagai hal yang bermanfaat, seperti senyawa kimia untuk obat-obatan.

questions.questionText: Which of the following is NOT listed as a primary driver of deforestation in the Amazon?
options: Cattle ranching
Large-scale agriculture
Urban sprawl and skyscraper construction
Logging
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'Urban sprawl and skyscraper construction'. Teks menyebutkan bahwa deforestasi utamanya didorong oleh pertanian skala besar, peternakan sapi (cattle ranching), dan penebangan kayu (logging). Pembangunan kota dan gedung pencakar langit tidak disebutkan sebagai pendorong utama.

questions.questionText: According to the passage, what happens to the carbon stored in trees when they are burned?
options: It turns into liquid that fertilizes the soil.
It is released back into the atmosphere as a greenhouse gas.
It is permanently destroyed and removed from the environment.
It is transformed into oxygen through a chemical reaction.
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'It is released back into the atmosphere as a greenhouse gas'. Ketika pohon dibakar, karbon yang tersimpan selama berabad-abad dilepaskan kembali ke atmosfer sebagai emisi gas rumah kaca.

questions.questionText: What is the "tipping point" mentioned in paragraph 3?
options: The moment when the Amazon River reaches its highest level.
A degree of degradation where the forest can no longer sustain itself.
The point where the Amazon becomes the world's largest economy.
A physical location where two major rivers meet.
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'A degree of degradation where the forest can no longer sustain itself'. 'Tipping point' (titik kritis) merujuk pada kondisi kerusakan lingkungan yang sangat parah sehingga hutan hujan tidak dapat lagi mempertahankan dirinya sendiri dan akan berubah menjadi sabana kering.

questions.questionText: The word "inextricably" in paragraph 4 is closest in meaning to:
options: Temporarily
In a way that cannot be separated
Only partially
In a confusing manner
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'In a way that cannot be separated'. Frasa 'inextricably linked' berarti terhubung secara sangat erat dan tidak dapat dipisahkan. Ini menunjukkan bahwa masa depan Amazon dan masa depan iklim global saling terkait erat.

questions.questionText: What is the author's primary purpose in writing this article?
options: To encourage people to move to South America for better economic opportunities.
To explain the global biological and environmental significance of the Amazon.
To argue that logging is necessary for the development of local economies.
To provide a detailed travel guide for tourists visiting the Amazon River.
questions.explanation: Jawaban yang benar adalah 'To explain the global biological and environmental significance of the Amazon'. Tujuan utama penulis adalah menjelaskan pentingnya ekosistem Amazon secara biologis (biodiversitas) dan lingkungan (peran dalam iklim global) serta ancaman yang dihadapinya.

passages.title: The Industrial Revolution: A Catalyst for Global Transformation
passages.content:
The Industrial Revolution, which began in Great Britain in the late 18th century and later spread to other parts of the world, represents one of the most profound turning points in human history. This period marked a fundamental shift from agrarian, handicraft-based economies to those dominated by machine manufacturing and the factory system. The transition was fueled by a series of technological innovations that fundamentally altered how goods were produced, how people lived, and how nations interacted. Prior to this era, most manufacturing took place in small-scale "cottage industries," where individuals used hand tools or simple machines to produce goods in their homes. The advent of new technologies, however, centralized production into large-scale factories, sparking a massive migration from rural areas to burgeoning industrial cities.

At the heart of the first Industrial Revolution was the steam engine, perfected by James Watt in the 1770s. This invention provided a reliable and portable source of power that could be used to drive machinery in various industries, most notably textiles and mining. Before the steam engine, factories were largely dependent on water power, which limited their location to sites near fast-flowing rivers. Steam power liberated factories from these geographic constraints, allowing them to be built anywhere, particularly near large labor pools or coal deposits. The textile industry was revolutionized by machines like the spinning jenny and the power loom, which dramatically increased the speed and efficiency of cloth production. This surge in productivity made manufactured goods more affordable and accessible to a wider segment of the population, fundamentally changing consumer behavior.

The revolution also transformed transportation and communication, further integrating global markets. The development of steam-powered locomotives and steamships allowed for the rapid movement of raw materials and finished goods over long distances. This led to the expansion of trade and the growth of a truly global economy. Similarly, the invention of the electric telegraph by Samuel Morse and others enabled near-instantaneous communication across vast stretches of land and sea, collapsing the time and space that had previously separated distant regions. These advancements facilitated the rise of powerful industrial nations, but they also led to significant social changes. The rapid growth of cities often outpaced the development of infrastructure, leading to overcrowded and unsanitary living conditions for the new industrial working class.

While the Industrial Revolution brought about unprecedented economic growth and technological progress, it also introduced complex social and environmental challenges. Factory work was often characterized by long hours, low wages, and dangerous working conditions, frequently involving the exploitation of child labor. These harsh realities sparked the rise of labor unions and social reform movements that sought to improve the lives of workers through legislation and collective bargaining. Furthermore, the reliance on fossil fuels, particularly coal, led to widespread air and water pollution, marking the beginning of the human-induced environmental changes that persist today. Despite these difficulties, the Industrial Revolution paved the way for the modern world, laying the foundation for the technological and economic structures that define the 21st century.

questions.questionText: What was the primary economic shift during the Industrial Revolution?
options: From factory-based production to cottage industries.
From machine manufacturing to agrarian-based economies.
From manual labor and handicraft to machine-led manufacturing.
From global trade to isolated local markets.
questions.explanation: Pergeseran ekonomi utama adalah dari sistem berbasis pertanian dan kerajinan tangan (cottage industries) ke sistem manufaktur yang didominasi oleh mesin dan pabrik.

questions.questionText: The word "burgeoning" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Declining
Rapidly growing
Peaceful
Ancient
questions.explanation: "Burgeoning" berarti berkembang pesat atau tumbuh dengan cepat, merujuk pada pertumbuhan kota-kota industri.

questions.questionText: How did the steam engine change the location of factories?
options: It required them to be built closer to the ocean for cooling.
It allowed them to be built away from rivers and near coal or labor.
It forced them to remain in rural areas to avoid city taxes.
It limited factories to locations with high wind speeds.
questions.explanation: Sebelumnya pabrik bergantung pada tenaga air (sungai). Tenaga uap membebaskan pabrik dari batasan geografis ini, memungkinkan mereka dibangun di mana saja, terutama dekat sumber batubara atau tempat berkumpulnya tenaga kerja.

questions.questionText: What was a direct result of increased productivity in the textile industry?
options: Cloth became much more expensive and rare.
People stopped buying manufactured goods altogether.
Manufactured goods became more affordable for many people.
The government banned the use of power looms.
questions.explanation: Peningkatan produktivitas membuat barang-barang manufaktur menjadi lebih murah dan mudah diakses oleh masyarakat luas, mengubah perilaku konsumen.

questions.questionText: What is the defining characteristic of "renewable energy" as described in the passage?
options: It is energy that cannot be replenished once it is used.
It is derived from processes that are replaced naturally and quickly.
It is only available in regions with very hot climates.
It is more expensive than fossil fuels under all circumstances.
questions.explanation: Energi terbarukan didefinisikan sebagai energi yang berasal dari proses alam yang diperbarui pada tingkat yang sama atau lebih cepat daripada saat energi tersebut dikonsumsi.

questions.questionText: The word "mitigate" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Increase the intensity of
Make less severe or serious
Completely ignore
Celebrate as a success
questions.explanation: "Mitigate" berarti mengurangi keparahan atau dampak negatif dari sesuatu, dalam konteks ini adalah mengurangi dampak perubahan iklim.

questions.questionText: Why is energy storage technology mentioned in relation to solar and wind energy?
options: Because these sources produce too much energy for the grid to handle.
Because the sun does not always shine and the wind does not always blow.
To prevent solar panels from overheating during the day.
Because batteries are required for the initial installation of any turbine.
questions.explanation: Karena energi surya dan angin bersifat intermiten (outputnya berubah-ubah tergantung cuaca), teknologi penyimpanan seperti baterai diperlukan untuk memastikan pasokan daya tetap stabil dan andal.

questions.questionText: The word "plummeted" in paragraph 2 suggests that the cost of solar technology has:
options: Increased slightly over time.
Remained exactly the same for decades.
Dropped sharply and rapidly.
Become impossible to calculate.
questions.explanation: "Plummeted" berarti jatuh atau turun dengan sangat tajam dan cepat, menunjukkan penurunan harga yang drastis.

questions.questionText: What is the main advantage of geothermal energy over solar or wind energy?
options: It is much easier to install in residential homes.
It provides a constant and reliable source of power.
It does not require any machinery or turbines.
It is only found in the middle of large oceans.
questions.explanation: Keunggulan energi panas bumi adalah kemampuannya menyediakan sumber listrik yang konstan dan andal (baseload power), berbeda dengan matahari atau angin yang bersifat intermiten.

questions.questionText: According to the passage, how do "run-of-river" hydroelectric systems differ from traditional dams?
options: They produce much more electricity than large-scale dams.
They require the construction of even larger reservoirs.
They generate power from natural water flow without massive reservoirs.
They can only be used in regions where it never rains.
questions.explanation: Sistem 'run-of-river' dirancang untuk meminimalkan dampak lingkungan dengan menghasilkan listrik dari aliran alami sungai tanpa memerlukan pembangunan waduk (reservoir) yang besar.

questions.questionText: The phrase "variable renewable power" in paragraph 4 refers to:
options: Energy sources like geothermal that never change.
The different colors of light captured by solar panels.
Sources like wind and solar whose output fluctuates.
The various prices of electricity in different countries.
questions.explanation: "Variable renewable power" mengacu pada sumber energi seperti angin dan matahari yang outputnya berfluktuasi atau berubah-ubah tergantung kondisi cuaca dan waktu.

questions.questionText: What is one environmental concern regarding the production of renewable energy technology?
options: The mining of raw materials for batteries and panels.
The excessive amount of oxygen released by wind turbines.
The noise pollution created by solar panels during the night.
The fact that renewable energy will lead to global cooling.
questions.explanation: Salah satu kekhawatiran lingkungan adalah dampak dari penambangan bahan mentah (seperti lithium dan material lainnya) yang dibutuhkan untuk membuat baterai dan panel surya.

questions.questionText: The word "inevitable" in paragraph 4 is closest in meaning to:
options: Unlikely to happen
Certain to happen; unavoidable
Already completed in the past
Highly dangerous for society
questions.explanation: "Inevitable" berarti sesuatu yang pasti terjadi atau tidak dapat dihindari. Penulis menyatakan bahwa transisi ini akan terjadi karena inovasi teknologi dan perubahan iklim.

questions.questionText: Which of the following best summarizes the author's primary argument?
options: Fossil fuels are still the most reliable and affordable energy source.
renewable energy is a perfect solution with no technical or social challenges.
The transition to renewable energy is necessary and gaining momentum despite obstacles.
International policy should focus exclusively on geothermal energy to save the planet.
questions.explanation: Argumen utama penulis adalah bahwa meskipun ada tantangan (infrastruktur, politik), transisi menuju energi terbarukan sangat penting dan momentumnya semakin kuat.

passages.title: Renewable Energy: Harvesting the Power of Nature
passages.content:
As the global community grapples with the dual challenges of rising energy demand and the urgent need to mitigate climate change, the transition from fossil fuels to renewable energy sources has become a central focus of international policy. Renewable energy, derived from natural processes that are replenished at a rate equal to or faster than they are consumed, offers a sustainable alternative to traditional energy sources like coal, oil, and natural gas. These non-renewable fuels, while having powered the Industrial Revolution and modern economic growth, are finite resources whose combustion releases significant amounts of greenhouse gases into the atmosphere. In contrast, technologies that harness solar, wind, hydroelectric, and geothermal power provide energy with significantly lower carbon footprints, promising a cleaner and more resilient future.

Solar energy, perhaps the most abundant renewable resource, involves capturing sunlight and converting it into electricity using photovoltaic (PV) cells or concentrated solar power systems. In recent years, the cost of solar technology has plummeted, making it increasingly competitive with conventional power generation. Similarly, wind energy has seen rapid expansion, with massive wind turbines being deployed both onshore and offshore to capture the kinetic energy of moving air. Innovations in turbine design and materials have allowed for larger, more efficient machines that can operate at lower wind speeds. Both solar and wind are intermittent energy sources, meaning their output varies depending on the weather and time of day. This has spurred significant research into energy storage technologies, such as advanced lithium-ion batteries and green hydrogen production, to ensure a stable and reliable power supply.

Hydroelectric power, which generates electricity by harnessing the energy of flowing or falling water, remains the largest source of renewable energy globally. Large-scale dams have long been used to provide consistent baseload power, although they can have significant environmental impacts on river ecosystems and local communities. Newer, "run-of-river" systems aim to minimize these impacts by generating power from the natural flow of the river without the need for massive reservoirs. Geothermal energy, another potent resource, taps into the heat generated from within the Earth’s core. In regions with high geothermal activity, such as Iceland and parts of the United States, this heat can be used to produce steam that drives turbines, providing a constant and reliable source of carbon-free electricity.

Despite the clear benefits of renewable energy, the transition is not without obstacles. Integrating high levels of variable renewable power into existing electrical grids requires significant upgrades to infrastructure and the development of "smart grids" that can manage complex, bi-directional energy flows. There are also concerns regarding the environmental impact of mining the raw materials needed for batteries and solar panels, as well as the land use requirements for large-scale renewable projects. Furthermore, political and economic interests tied to the fossil fuel industry often present barriers to rapid reform. However, as technological innovation continues to drive down costs and the physical realities of climate change become increasingly apparent, the momentum toward a 100 percent renewable energy future appears inevitable, marking a new era in humanity's relationship with the natural world.

questions.questionText: What is the defining characteristic of "renewable energy" as described in the passage?
options: It is energy that cannot be replenished once it is used.
It is derived from processes that are replaced naturally and quickly.
It is only available in regions with very hot climates.
It is more expensive than fossil fuels under all circumstances.
questions.explanation: Ciri khas energi terbarukan adalah bahwa ia berasal dari proses alam yang diperbarui pada tingkat yang sama atau lebih cepat daripada laju konsumsinya.

questions.questionText: The word "mitigate" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Increase the intensity of
Make less severe or serious
Completely ignore
Celebrate as a success
questions.explanation: "Mitigate" berarti mengurangi keparahan atau dampak negatif dari sesuatu, dalam konteks ini adalah upaya untuk mengurangi dampak perubahan iklim.

questions.questionText: Why is energy storage technology mentioned in relation to solar and wind energy?
options: Because these sources produce too much energy for the grid to handle.
Because the sun does not always shine and the wind does not always blow.
To prevent solar panels from overheating during the day.
Because batteries are required for the initial installation of any turbine.
questions.explanation: Teknologi penyimpanan diperlukan karena sumber energi surya dan angin bersifat intermiten (tidak stabil), sehingga teknologi ini memastikan pasokan daya tetap stabil dan dapat diandalkan.

questions.questionText: The word "plummeted" in paragraph 2 suggests that the cost of solar technology has:
options: Increased slightly over time.
Remained exactly the same for decades.
Dropped sharply and rapidly.
Become impossible to calculate.
questions.explanation: "Plummeted" berarti turun atau jatuh dengan sangat tajam dan cepat, yang menunjukkan penurunan biaya teknologi surya yang signifikan.

questions.questionText: What is the main advantage of geothermal energy over solar or wind energy?
options: It is much easier to install in residential homes.
It provides a constant and reliable source of power.
It does not require any machinery or turbines.
It is only found in the middle of large oceans.
questions.explanation: Keunggulan energi panas bumi adalah kemampuannya menyediakan sumber listrik yang konstan dan andal (baseload power), berbeda dengan energi matahari atau angin yang intermiten.

questions.questionText: According to the passage, how do "run-of-river" hydroelectric systems differ from traditional dams?
options: They produce much more electricity than large-scale dams.
They require the construction of even larger reservoirs.
They generate power from natural water flow without massive reservoirs.
They can only be used in regions where it never rains.
questions.explanation: Sistem 'run-of-river' berbeda karena mereka menghasilkan listrik dari aliran air alami sungai tanpa perlu membangun waduk raksasa.

questions.questionText: The phrase "variable renewable power" in paragraph 4 refers to:
options: Energy sources like geothermal that never change.
The different colors of light captured by solar panels.
Sources like wind and solar whose output fluctuates.
The various prices of electricity in different countries.
questions.explanation: "Variable renewable power" merujuk pada sumber energi seperti angin dan matahari yang outputnya berfluktuasi atau berubah-ubah tergantung kondisi cuaca.

questions.questionText: What is one environmental concern regarding the production of renewable energy technology?
options: The mining of raw materials for batteries and panels.
The excessive amount of oxygen released by wind turbines.
The noise pollution created by solar panels during the night.
The fact that renewable energy will lead to global cooling.
questions.explanation: Salah satu kekhawatiran lingkungan terkait teknologi energi terbarukan adalah dampak lingkungan dari penambangan bahan mentah yang diperlukan untuk baterai dan panel surya.

questions.questionText: The word "inevitable" in paragraph 4 is closest in meaning to:
options: Unlikely to happen
Certain to happen; unavoidable
Already completed in the past
Highly dangerous for society
questions.explanation: "Inevitable" berarti sesuatu yang pasti terjadi atau tidak dapat dihindari. Penulis menyimpulkan bahwa transisi ini adalah hal yang tak terelakkan.

questions.questionText: Which of the following best summarizes the author's primary argument?
options: Fossil fuels are still the most reliable and affordable energy source.
renewable energy is a perfect solution with no technical or social challenges.
The transition to renewable energy is necessary and gaining momentum despite obstacles.
International policy should focus exclusively on geothermal energy to save the planet.
questions.explanation: Poin utama penulis adalah bahwa meskipun ada tantangan (infrastruktur, politik), dorongan menuju energi terbarukan untuk masa depan yang lebih bersih adalah suatu keharusan yang tidak dapat dihindari.

passages.title: The Silk Road: An Ancient Network of Cultural Exchange
passages.content:
The Silk Road, a vast network of interconnected trade routes stretching across the Eurasian continent for over 6,000 kilometers, served as the primary conduit for cultural and economic exchange between the East and the West for more than 1,500 years. Named by the 19th-century German geographer Ferdinand von Richthofen, the "Silk Road" was never a single path but rather a complex web of land and sea routes that linked the civilizations of China, India, Persia, Arabia, and the Mediterranean. This historical artery permitted not only the movement of precious commodities like silk, spices, and precious metals but also facilitated the spread of religions, philosophies, scientific knowledge, and artistic styles that fundamentally shaped the development of the modern world.

As the name suggests, silk was one of the most highly prized goods transported along these routes. For centuries, the secret of silk production was closely guarded by the Chinese, giving them a lucrative monopoly on a fabric that became a symbol of wealth and status in the Roman Empire and beyond. However, the Silk Road was far from a one-way street. Merchants traveling eastward brought with them horses, grapes, glassware, and Western artistic influences. Central Asian kingdoms acted as vital intermediaries, establishing prosperous oasis cities such as Samarkand and Bukhara that became centers of learning and cosmopolitan culture. The exchange was not limited to material wealth; the constant interaction between diverse peoples led to a rich syncretism, where ideas from different cultures were blended to create new forms of expression.

Perhaps the most significant impact of the Silk Road was its role in the transmission of major world religions. Buddhism, originating in India, traveled along the northern trade routes into China and eventually reached Korea and Japan. Similarly, Nestorian Christianity, Manichaeism, and later Islam spread through the network, often adapting to the local customs of the regions they encountered. This religious diffusion was accompanied by the spread of scientific and technological innovations. Paper-making, gunpowder, and the compass—three of the "Four Great Inventions" of ancient China—gradually made their way to Europe through the Islamic world, where they eventually ignited the intellectual transformations of the Renaissance and the Age of Discovery.

The decline of the Silk Road in the 14th and 15th centuries was precipitated by several factors, including the disintegration of the Mongol Empire and the rise of maritime trade routes. As European explorers like Vasco da Gama discovered direct sea routes to Asia, the dangerous and time-consuming overland paths became less economically viable. Political instability and the outbreak of the Black Death further hindered travel and commerce along the ancient network. Although the routes eventually fell into disuse, the legacy of the Silk Road persists as a testament to humanity's enduring desire for connection and exchange. Today, the concept of a "New Silk Road" or the "Belt and Road Initiative" reflects a modern effort to revitalize these historical connections, underscoring the timeless importance of cross-cultural cooperation in an increasingly globalized society.

questions.questionText: What does the author emphasize about the physical nature of the Silk Road?
options: it was a single, well-paved road that crossed only one country.
It was a complex and interconnected network of multiple routes.
It was primarily a series of underground tunnels used by soldiers.
It was a maritime path that never touched land.
questions.explanation: Penulis menekankan bahwa Jalur Sutra bukanlah satu jalan tunggal, melainkan jaringan kompleks rute darat dan laut yang saling terhubung.

questions.questionText: The word "conduit" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Barrier
Channel or mean of transmission
Type of expensive fabric
Ancient religious leader
questions.explanation: "Conduit" berarti saluran atau sarana untuk mentransfer atau mengirimkan sesuatu, di sini merujuk pada jalur pertukaran budaya dan ekonomi.

questions.questionText: Why did the Chinese have a monopoly on silk production for centuries?
options: No other country had the right kind of soil to grow silk.
They kept the techniques for making silk a secret.
Silk could only be produced by royal families in China.
The Roman Empire banned the production of silk in Europe.
questions.explanation: Teks menjelaskan bahwa monopoli Tiongkok didasarkan pada fakta bahwa mereka menjaga rahasia teknik produksi sutra secara ketat.

questions.questionText: What role did Central Asian kingdoms like Samarkand play in the Silk Road?
options: They were isolationist regions that refused to trade with others.
They served as important intermediaries and centers of culture.
They were the primary producers of all the silk sold in the West.
They were responsible for building the first sea routes to Europe.
questions.explanation: Kerajaan-kerajaan di Asia Tengah berfungsi sebagai perantara vital yang juga menjadi pusat pembelajaran dan budaya kosmopolitan sepanjang Jalur Sutra.

questions.questionText: The word "syncretism" in paragraph 2 refers to:
options: The mechanical process of weaving silk cloth.
The blending of different beliefs or cultures.
A type of currency used by ancient merchants.
The conflict between two powerful empires.
questions.explanation: "Syncretism" merujuk pada percampuran atau peleburan berbagai ide, kepercayaan, dan budaya untuk menghasilkan bentuk ekspresi baru.

questions.questionText: Which major religion is mentioned as spreading from India to East Asia via the Silk Road?
options: Islam
Buddhism
Christianity
Manichaeism
questions.explanation: Agama Buddha, yang berasal dari India, adalah agama utama yang menyebar melalui rute perdagangan ke Tiongkok dan Asia Timur lainnya.
