questions.explanation: Jika arus jet stream melemah, ia dapat menyebabkan cuaca menjadi 'stagnan' (tidak bergerak), yang berpotensi menghasilkan kondisi ekstrem seperti gelombang panas yang berkepanjangan atau badai dingin yang ekstrem.

questions.questionText: The author characterizes the ice-albedo feedback as a "self-reinforcing cycle" because:
options: It eventually stops on its own.
The result of the process causes the process to continue and intensify.
It is a cycle that only happens once every century.
It helps cool the Earth during the summer months.
questions.explanation: Siklus 'self-reinforcing' (memperkuat diri) berarti bahwa hasil dari suatu tindakan (misalnya, es mencair) mempercepat tindakan itu sendiri. Dalam kasus albedo, pencairan menyebabkan penyerapan panas yang lebih besar, yang kemudian menyebabkan pencairan lebih lanjut, sehingga siklus tersebut semakin intensif.

questions.questionText: Which of the following can be inferred about light-colored surfaces?
options: They are more dangerous for the environment than dark surfaces.
They contribute to global warming by absorbing solar energy.
They help regulate Earth's temperature by reflecting heat.
They have a very low albedo value.
questions.explanation: Karena permukaan terang memiliki albedo tinggi, mereka memantulkan sebagian besar energi matahari kembali ke luar angkasa. Hal ini berfungsi sebagai mekanisme pengaturan suhu dengan mencegah panas diserap oleh permukaan Bumi.

questions.questionText: The word "stagnant" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
options: Rapidly changing
Not moving or flowing
Extremely cold
Very windy
questions.explanation: "Stagnant" berarti diam, tidak bergerak, atau tidak mengalir. Dalam konteks cuaca, ini merujuk pada kondisi di mana udara tidak bergerak, yang menyebabkan kondisi cuaca menjadi tidak stabil dan berkepanjangan.

questions.questionText: The passage mentions all of the following as consequences of Arctic sea ice loss EXCEPT:
options: Changes in the jet stream.
Increased absorption of heat by the ocean.
Faster warming at the North Pole.
An immediate increase in solar radiation from the sun.
questions.explanation: Kehilangan es laut menyebabkan perubahan pada jet stream (A), meningkatkan penyerapan panas oleh lautan (B), dan mempercepat pemanasan di Kutub Utara (C). Namun, peningkatan radiasi matahari dari matahari itu sendiri adalah faktor eksternal yang tidak disebabkan oleh hilangnya es laut.

passages.title: The Psychology of Cognitive Dissonance
passages.content:
Cognitive dissonance is a psychological phenomenon that occurs when an individual holds two or more contradictory beliefs, ideas, or values simultaneously. First proposed by Leon Festinger in 1957, the theory suggests that this inconsistency causes a state of mental discomfort, which motivates the person to reduce the dissonance. To alleviate this psychological tension, individuals may change their beliefs, acquire new information that outweighs the dissonant beliefs, or justify their behavior by minimizing the importance of the conflict.

A classic example of cognitive dissonance is found in the behavior of smokers who are aware that smoking is detrimental to their health. The knowledge that "smoking causes cancer" conflicts with the behavior of "smoking." To reduce the resulting discomfort, a smoker might utilize several strategies: they might convince themselves that the health risks are exaggerated, focus on the short-term stress-relief benefits of smoking, or point to an elderly relative who smoked for decades without illness as a way to rationalize their choice. This innate drive for internal consistency can often lead to irrational decision-making and a resistance to changing harmful habits, as the mind prioritizes the avoidance of discomfort over objective truth.

questions.questionText: What is the core definition of cognitive dissonance?
options: A state of extreme happiness caused by achieving a goal.
Mental discomfort resulting from conflicting beliefs or behaviors.
The ability to memorize large amounts of contradictory data.
A physical illness caused by environmental stress.
questions.explanation: Disonansi kognitif adalah ketidaknyamanan mental (mental discomfort) yang dirasakan individu ketika mereka memegang dua atau lebih keyakinan, ide, atau nilai yang bertentangan secara bersamaan.

questions.questionText: The word "alleviate" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Increase
Relieve
Ignore
Document
questions.explanation: "Alleviate" berarti meredakan atau mengurangi. Dalam konteks teori ini, individu berusaha untuk meredakan ketegangan psikologis yang timbul dari disonansi kognitif.

questions.questionText: According to the theory, how do people typically react to cognitive dissonance?
options: They embrace the discomfort as a learning opportunity.
They seek to increase the number of contradictory beliefs they hold.
They are motivated to reduce the inconsistency to regain mental comfort.
They immediately forget one of the conflicting ideas.
questions.explanation: Teori ini menyatakan bahwa disonansi kognitif memotivasi individu untuk mengurangi ketidaksesuaian tersebut. Tujuannya adalah untuk mencapai konsistensi internal dan kembali ke keadaan kenyamanan mental.

questions.questionText: The word "detrimental" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
options: Beneficial
Neutral
Harmful
Inexpensive
questions.explanation: "Detrimental" berarti merugikan atau berbahaya. Dalam konteks kesehatan, merokok dianggap merugikan kesehatan.

questions.questionText: In the smoker example, pointing to an elderly relative who smokes is an instance of:
options: Changing the behavior to match the belief.
Rationalizing a behavior to reduce mental tension.
Acquiring objective scientific data.
Increasing the state of cognitive dissonance.
questions.explanation: Mencari pembenaran (rationalize) perilaku buruk dengan mencari contoh yang mendukung (seperti kerabat yang merokok) adalah strategi untuk mengurangi ketegangan disonansi kognitif.

questions.questionText: The word "innate" in paragraph 2 means:
options: Learned through education
Inborn or natural
Very slow
Unimportant
questions.explanation: "Innate" berarti bawaan atau alami. Dalam konteks ini, dorongan untuk konsistensi internal adalah sifat psikologis yang sudah ada sejak lahir.

questions.questionText: What does the passage suggest about the mind's priorities during cognitive dissonance?
options: It prioritizes objective facts over emotional comfort.
It prioritizes avoiding mental discomfort over acknowledging reality.
It seeks to find the most logical and scientific solution possible.
It prioritizes physical health over psychological stability.
questions.explanation: Penulis menyimpulkan bahwa dorongan bawaan pikiran adalah menghindari ketidaknyamanan mental (discomfort) daripada mengakui kebenaran objektif, yang sering mengarah pada pengambilan keputusan yang tidak rasional.

questions.questionText: All of the following are mentioned as ways to reduce dissonance EXCEPT:
options: Changing one's beliefs.
Minimizing the importance of the conflict.
Seeking out more contradictory information.
Justifying the behavior.
questions.explanation: Mencari informasi yang lebih kontradiktif (seeking out more contradictory information) justru akan meningkatkan disonansi kognitif, bukan menguranginya. Cara untuk mengurangi disonansi adalah dengan mengubah keyakinan, meminimalkan konflik, atau membenarkan perilaku.

questions.questionText: The tone of the author can best be described as:
options: Highly emotional and judgmental.
Objective and explanatory.
Sarcastic and dismissive.
Angry and confrontational.
questions.explanation: Penulis menyajikan teori psikologis (cognitive dissonance) dan contohnya secara netral dan informatif. Oleh karena itu, nadanya bersifat objektif dan bertujuan untuk menjelaskan konsep tersebut.

questions.questionText: Cognitive dissonance theory helps explain why:
options: People find it easy to admit when they are wrong.
People often resist changing unhealthy habits despite knowing the risks.
Humans have evolved to have superior memory.
Smoking is more addictive than other behaviors.
questions.explanation: Teori ini menjelaskan mengapa orang sering kali menolak untuk mengubah kebiasaan buruk (misalnya merokok) meskipun mereka sadar akan risiko negatifnya, karena mereka memilih untuk merasionalisasi perilaku tersebut daripada menghadapi ketidaknyamanan mental.

passages.title: The Evolution of Gothic Architecture
passages.content:
The transition from Romanesque to Gothic architecture in 12th-century Europe marked a revolutionary shift in engineering and aesthetics. Romanesque buildings were characterized by thick walls, small windows, and rounded arches, which limited their height and interior light. Gothic architects, however, sought to create structures that were taller and more luminous, symbolizing a divine connection. This was made possible by three key innovations: the pointed arch, the ribbed vault, and the flying buttress.

The pointed arch redistributed weight more effectively than the rounded arch, allowing for much taller ceilings. The ribbed vault provided a structural framework that supported the roof's weight while using less stone. Most importantly, the flying buttress—an external support beam—carried the lateral thrust of the walls outward, which meant that walls no longer had to be thick to support the roof. This allowed for the installation of massive stained-glass windows, transforming cathedrals into spaces filled with ethereal light and color. Gothic cathedrals like Chartres and Notre-Dame de Paris stand as testaments to this era's technical prowess and artistic vision.

questions.questionText: What was a primary limitation of Romanesque architecture?
options: It used too much stained glass.
The walls were too thin to support the weight.
Thick walls and small windows limited light and height.
Rounded arches made the buildings too tall.
questions.explanation: Arsitektur Romanesque dibatasi oleh penggunaan dinding yang tebal dan jendela yang kecil serta lengkungan bulat (rounded arches), yang secara signifikan membatasi ketinggian bangunan dan jumlah cahaya alami yang masuk.

questions.questionText: The word "luminous" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Heavy
Dark
Bright; full of light
Religious
questions.explanation: "Luminous" berarti memancarkan cahaya atau terang. Dalam konteks arsitektur Gothic, ini merujuk pada bangunan yang penuh dengan cahaya dan warna, terutama dari jendela kaca patri.

questions.questionText: How did the pointed arch improve upon the rounded arch?
options: It was easier to build with wood.
It redistributed weight more effectively for greater height.
It required thicker walls for stability.
It eliminated the need for windows.
questions.explanation: Lengkungan runcing (pointed arch) memiliki kemampuan teknik yang lebih baik untuk mendistribusikan berat struktur ke bawah dan ke samping, sehingga memungkinkan para arsitek membangun bangunan dengan langit-langit yang jauh lebih tinggi.

questions.questionText: The word "thrust" in paragraph 2 refers to:
options: A type of decorative paint.
The outward pressure or force exerted by a structure.
The speed of the construction process.
The religious meaning of the building.
questions.explanation: "Thrust" dalam istilah arsitektur merujuk pada tekanan atau gaya dorong keluar (lateral thrust) yang dihasilkan oleh beban atap dan dinding, yang harus ditahan oleh struktur pendukung.

questions.questionText: What was the critical function of the flying buttress?
options: To decorate the outside of the building with statues.
To carry the weight of the walls outward, allowing for thinner walls.
To prevent light from entering the cathedral.
To support the foundation of the building from underground.
questions.explanation: Fungsi utama flying buttress adalah untuk menahan gaya dorong lateral (lateral thrust) dari dinding ke arah luar. Dengan melakukan ini, dinding tidak lagi perlu tebal, memungkinkan pemasangan jendela kaca patri yang besar.

questions.questionText: The word "ethereal" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
options: Heavy and solid
Extremely delicate or heavenly
Dark and gloomy
Cheap and artificial
questions.explanation: "Ethereal" berarti sangat halus, ringan, atau surgawi (heavenly). Dalam konteks ini, ia menggambarkan suasana cahaya dan warna yang tampak melayang dan ilahi di dalam katedral.

questions.questionText: Why were Gothic architects able to include large stained-glass windows?
options: Because the flying buttress took over the structural role of thick walls.
Because they discovered how to make glass stronger than stone.
Because they wanted the buildings to be as dark as possible.
Because the Romanesque style had already used them extensively.
questions.explanation: Jendela kaca patri besar dimungkinkan karena inovasi struktural seperti flying buttress mengambil alih peran penyangga dinding, sehingga dinding bisa dibuat lebih tipis dan memungkinkan jendela yang lebih besar.

questions.questionText: The passage implies that Gothic architecture was driven by both:
options: Political power and military strategy.
Engineering innovation and spiritual symbolism.
Scientific atheism and Roman history.
Economic greed and available stone.
questions.explanation: Arsitektur Gothic didorong oleh dua faktor utama: inovasi teknik (pointed arch, ribbed vault, flying buttress) dan tujuan simbolis spiritualnya—yaitu menciptakan ruang yang bercahaya untuk menunjukkan koneksi ilahi.

questions.questionText: The word "prowess" in the final sentence means:
options: Failure
Superior skill or ability
Ancient history
Financial cost
questions.explanation: "Prowess" berarti keahlian, kecakapan, atau kemampuan yang luar biasa. Kata ini digunakan untuk menggambarkan keterampilan teknis dan visi artistik para arsitek Gothic.

questions.questionText: Which of the following buildings is NOT used as an example of Gothic architecture?
options: Notre-Dame de Paris
Chartres Cathedral
The Romanesque Basilica
All of the above are Gothic.
questions.explanation: Basilika Romanesque adalah contoh dari gaya arsitektur sebelumnya (Romanesque), bukan Gothic. Notre-Dame de Paris dan Chartres Cathedral adalah contoh utama dari arsitektur Gothic.

passages.title: The Great Oxidation Event
passages.content:
Approximately 2.4 billion years ago, Earth underwent one of its most transformative biological and geological events: the Great Oxidation Event (GOE). Before this period, Earth's atmosphere was predominantly composed of methane and carbon dioxide, with virtually no free oxygen. The catalyst for this change was the evolution of cyanobacteria—microscopic organisms capable of oxygenic photosynthesis. As these bacteria proliferated in the oceans, they began releasing oxygen as a byproduct, which initially reacted with dissolved iron to form massive rust deposits on the ocean floor.

Once the iron 'sinks' were saturated, free oxygen began to accumulate in the atmosphere. This shift was catastrophic for the majority of Earth's existing life forms, which were anaerobic (non-oxygen using) and found oxygen to be highly toxic. This period is often referred to as the 'Oxygen Holocaust,' marking a massive extinction event. However, the rise of oxygen also paved the way for the evolution of aerobic respiration, a much more efficient way to produce energy. This eventually allowed for the development of complex, multicellular organisms, including animals and humans. The GOE illustrates how a single biological innovation can fundamentally reshape the entire planet's chemistry and future evolutionary trajectory.

questions.questionText: What was the primary composition of Earth's atmosphere before the GOE?
options: Pure oxygen and nitrogen.
Methane and carbon dioxide.
Helium and hydrogen.
Water vapor and ozone.
questions.explanation: Sebelum Great Oxidation Event (GOE), atmosfer Bumi didominasi oleh gas metana (methane) dan karbon dioksida (carbon dioxide), dengan kandungan oksigen bebas yang sangat minim.

questions.questionText: The word "catalyst" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Result
Obstacle
Agent of change
Scientific tool
questions.explanation: "Catalyst" adalah zat atau proses yang bertindak sebagai pemicu atau agen perubahan. Dalam konteks ini, evolusi sianobakteri adalah agen yang memulai perubahan kimia atmosfer Bumi.

questions.questionText: How did cyanobacteria contribute to the Great Oxidation Event?
options: By consuming all the methane in the air.
By producing oxygen through photosynthesis.
By creating massive iron deposits through breathing.
By cooling the Earth's core.
questions.explanation: Sianobakteri adalah organisme yang mampu melakukan fotosintesis oksigenik, yaitu proses di mana mereka menghasilkan oksigen sebagai produk sampingan. Pelepasan oksigen inilah yang memicu GOE.

questions.questionText: What happened to the oxygen initially produced by cyanobacteria?
options: It escaped into outer space immediately.
It reacted with dissolved iron in the oceans.
It was used by anaerobic organisms for energy.
It turned the atmosphere into solid ice.
questions.explanation: Oksigen yang baru dihasilkan oleh sianobakteri awalnya bereaksi dengan zat besi terlarut (dissolved iron) di lautan dan membentuk endapan karat (rust deposits) di dasar laut.

questions.questionText: The word "saturated" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
options: Empty
Completely filled
Very cold
Toxic
questions.explanation: "Saturated" berarti jenuh atau terisi penuh. Ini mengacu pada titik di mana semua zat besi terlarut telah bereaksi dan membentuk endapan karat yang maksimal.

questions.questionText: Why is the GOE sometimes called the 'Oxygen Holocaust'?
options: Because it caused a massive extinction of anaerobic life.
Because it involved large-scale forest fires.
Because it made the Earth too hot for survival.
Because it destroyed the cyanobacteria themselves.
questions.explanation: Disebut 'Oxygen Holocaust' karena lonjakan oksigen yang tiba-tiba bersifat toksik dan menyebabkan kepunahan massal (massive extinction event) bagi sebagian besar kehidupan di Bumi yang bersifat anaerob.

questions.questionText: What was the long-term benefit of the accumulation of atmospheric oxygen?
options: It eliminated the greenhouse effect forever.
It allowed for the evolution of more efficient aerobic respiration.
It prevented further mutations in DNA.
It turned all methane into nutrients for plants.
questions.explanation: Meskipun awalnya toksik, manfaat jangka panjang dari oksigen adalah memfasilitasi evolusi respirasi aerobik, yang merupakan cara yang jauh lebih efisien untuk menghasilkan energi dan memungkinkan perkembangan organisme multiseluler kompleks.

questions.questionText: The word "trajectory" in the final sentence refers to:
options: A biological mutation.
The path or direction of development.
A type of chemical bond.
The speed of light.
questions.explanation: "Trajectory" merujuk pada jalur atau arah perkembangan. Dalam konteks ini, ini mengacu pada bagaimana evolusi kehidupan dan kimia planet berkembang di masa depan.

questions.questionText: The author implies that multicellular life:
options: Existed long before the GOE began.
Would likely not have evolved without the presence of oxygen.
Is less efficient than anaerobic life.
Was responsible for the creation of methane.
questions.explanation: Penulis menyiratkan bahwa kehidupan multiseluler (aerob) tidak akan berkembang secara signifikan tanpa adanya oksigen, karena oksigen memungkinkan respirasi aerobik yang efisien.

questions.questionText: Which statement best summarizes the main idea of the passage?
options: Methane is the most important gas for the survival of complex life.
The evolution of cyanobacteria led to a global chemical change that caused both extinction and the rise of complex life.
Rust deposits in the ocean are the primary source of Earth's modern energy.
Anaerobic organisms will eventually return to dominate the Earth's atmosphere.
questions.explanation: Ringkasan terbaik adalah bahwa evolusi sianobakteri memicu perubahan kimia global (GOE) yang memiliki konsekuensi ganda: kepunahan kehidupan anaerob dan memungkinkan munculnya kehidupan kompleks melalui respirasi aerobik.

passages.title: The Maritime Silk Road and the Sriwijaya Empire
passages.content:
The ascent of the Sriwijaya Empire between the 7th and 11th centuries represents a pivotal era in Southeast Asian maritime history. Strategically situated along the Melaka Straits, Sriwijaya functioned not merely as a political entity but as a formidable thalassocracy—a state whose power is derived primarily from its naval prowess and control over sea-based trade. By establishing a hegemony over the vital corridors linking the Indian Ocean to the South China Sea, Sriwijaya became the indispensable intermediary for the burgeoning trade between the Tang Dynasty of China and the Abbasid Caliphate in the Middle East.

Central to Sriwijaya's influence was its dual role as a commercial hub and a center for Buddhist erudition. The empire’s capital, likely located near modern-day Palembang, attracted scholars from across the Buddhist world, including the famous Chinese monk I-Tsing, who noted that the city housed over a thousand monks studying Sanskrit and sacred scriptures. This intellectual prestige bolstered the empire's legitimacy, allowing it to maintain a complex network of tributary relationships with local datus (chiefs) across Sumatra, the Malay Peninsula, and Western Java. However, the empire's reliance on maritime monopolies eventually rendered it vulnerable. The Chola raid from Southern India in 1025 CE severely debilitated Sriwijaya’s naval defenses, leading to a fragmented political landscape that paved the way for the eventual rise of the Majapahit Empire.

questions.questionText: What is the primary characteristic of a "thalassocracy" as described in the passage?
options: A government ruled by a religious hierarchy.
A state whose power is based on maritime and naval dominance.
A society that relies exclusively on land-based agriculture.
A decentralized confederation of mountain tribes.
questions.explanation: Thalassocracy didefinisikan sebagai negara yang kekuasaannya bersumber dari kekuatan angkatan laut dan kontrol atas perdagangan laut. Pilihan (B) paling akurat mencerminkan definisi ini. Pilihan lain salah karena tidak relevan dengan konsep thalassocracy.

questions.questionText: The word "formidable" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Inspiring fear or respect through size or power.
Easily defeated or weak.
Friendly and cooperative.
Ancient and forgotten.
questions.explanation: "Formidable" berarti sangat kuat, tangguh, atau mengesankan (inspiring respect/fear). Oleh karena itu, maknanya adalah menginspirasi rasa takut atau hormat melalui ukuran atau kekuasaan. Pilihan lain bertentangan dengan makna kata tersebut.

questions.questionText: Which of the following was a reason for Sriwijaya's importance in international trade?
options: Its production of advanced Chinese porcelain.
Its geographic location along the Melaka Straits.
Its alliance with the Roman Empire.
Its invention of the first magnetic compass.
questions.explanation: Pentingnya Sriwijaya dalam perdagangan internasional adalah karena lokasi strategisnya di Selat Malaka (Melaka Straits), yang menjadikannya perantara vital antara India/Timur Tengah dan Tiongkok. Pilihan lain tidak disebutkan sebagai faktor utama.

questions.questionText: The word "erudition" in paragraph 2 refers to:
options: Extensive knowledge or learning.
Military training.
Artistic performance.
Economic wealth.
questions.explanation: "Erudition" merujuk pada pengetahuan yang luas atau kecerdasan akademis. Ini terkait dengan peran Sriwijaya sebagai pusat studi agama dan literatur. Pilihan lain tidak sesuai dengan makna erudition.

questions.questionText: What can be inferred about the Chinese monk I-Tsing's stay in Sriwijaya?
options: He was unhappy with the quality of Buddhist education there.
He found the city to be an important center for linguistic and religious study.
He was the first person to introduce Buddhism to the empire.
He traveled there to lead a military rebellion against the datus.
questions.explanation: Catatan I-Tsing mengenai ribuan biksu yang belajar Sanskerta menunjukkan bahwa Sriwijaya adalah pusat studi bahasa dan agama Buddha. Ini menyiratkan bahwa ia menemukan kota itu penting sebagai pusat studi keilmuan.

questions.questionText: The word "bolstered" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
options: Weakened
Strengthened or supported
Ignored
Replaced
questions.explanation: "Bolstered" berarti memperkuat atau mendukung. Dalam konteks ini, prestise intelektual (erudition) tersebut berfungsi untuk memperkuat legitimasi kekaisaran Sriwijaya. Pilihan (B) adalah padanan yang tepat.

questions.questionText: According to the passage, the Chola raid of 1025 CE was significant because it:
options: Introduced Buddhism to Southern India.
Weakened Sriwijaya's naval power and stability.
Ended the trade between China and the Middle East.
Formally established Palembang as the capital.
questions.explanation: Teks menyatakan bahwa serangan Chola secara serius melemahkan pertahanan angkatan laut Sriwijaya, yang menyebabkan fragmentasi politik. Pilihan (B) adalah ringkasan dampak utama dari peristiwa tersebut.

questions.questionText: The term "tributary relationships" in the passage suggests that:
options: Local chiefs paid respect or taxes to Sriwijaya in exchange for protection or trade access.
Sriwijaya was a democracy where every chief had equal power.
The empire was constantly at war with all of its neighbors.
Trade was conducted without any form of government intervention.
questions.explanation: Hubungan upeti (tributary relationships) menunjukkan bahwa para datu lokal mengakui kekuasaan pusat Sriwijaya, kemungkinan besar dalam bentuk pengakuan politik atau pembayaran upeti sebagai imbalan atas perlindungan atau akses perdagangan. Pilihan (A) paling tepat.

questions.questionText: The word "debilitated" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
options: Improved
Enfeebled or weakened
Expanded
Protected
questions.explanation: "Debilitated" berarti melemahkan atau membuat tidak berdaya. Serangan Chola menyebabkan Sriwijaya menjadi lemah dan tidak stabil secara politik. Pilihan (B) adalah padanan yang tepat.

questions.questionText: Which statement best describes the structure of the passage?
options: A chronological list of all Sriwijayan kings.
An analysis of the empire's rise, its dual significance, and the factors leading to its decline.
A comparison between Sriwijaya and the Majapahit Empire.
A detailed description of Buddhist architecture in Palembang.
questions.explanation: Teks ini tidak hanya menjelaskan sejarah Sriwijaya tetapi juga menganalisis kebangkitannya (perdagangan), signifikansi gandanya (ekonomi dan budaya/erudition), serta faktor-faktor yang menyebabkan kemundurannya (serangan Chola). Pilihan (B) adalah deskripsi struktur yang paling tepat.

passages.title: Wayang Kulit: Shadow Puppetry as a Cultural Synthesis
passages.content:
Wayang Kulit, the traditional shadow puppetry of Indonesia, particularly on the islands of Java and Bali, is far more than a form of entertainment; it is a sophisticated medium for moral instruction, social commentary, and spiritual reflection. The term 'wayang' is derived from the Javanese word for 'shadow' or 'spirit,' and the performances involve intricately carved leather puppets cast against a taut linen screen. Central to this art form is the dalang, a master puppeteer who must possess an extraordinary range of skills: he manipulates the puppets, provides all the voices, directs the gamelan orchestra, and improvises philosophical discourse suited to the contemporary audience.

The narratives of Wayang Kulit primarily draw from the ancient Indian epics, the Ramayana and the Mahabharata. However, these stories have undergone a significant cultural synthesis over centuries, incorporating indigenous Javanese deities, folklore, and local wisdom. For instance, the introduction of the Punokawan—clown-servant characters like Semar—provides a distinctly Javanese perspective, often serving as the voice of the common people to critique social injustices. This adaptability has allowed Wayang Kulit to persist through various religious transitions in Indonesia, from Hindu-Buddhist influences to the Islamic era, where the art form was utilized by the Wali Songo (nine saints) as a tool for proselytization. By subtly weaving religious teachings into familiar stories, the saints ensured that the transition to Islam was culturally harmonious rather than disruptive.

questions.questionText: The word "sophisticated" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Simple and primitive.
Highly complex and refined.
Extremely loud.
Recent and modern.
questions.explanation: "Sophisticated" dalam konteks ini berarti sangat kompleks dan halus/terpoles (refined). Seni ini dianggap canggih karena melibatkan banyak elemen teknis yang rumit. Pilihan (B) adalah padanan yang tepat.

questions.questionText: According to the passage, what is a "dalang" required to do?
options: Only carve the leather puppets.
Manage the financial aspects of the performance.
Perform voices, manipulate puppets, and direct the music.
Remain silent throughout the entire performance.
questions.explanation: Teks menjelaskan bahwa dalang adalah master puppeteer yang harus melakukan banyak hal: memanipulasi wayang, memberikan suara, mengarahkan orkestra gamelan, dan berimprovisasi. Pilihan (C) mencakup semua peran tersebut.

questions.questionText: The word "synthesis" in paragraph 2 refers to:
options: The destruction of old ideas.
The combination of different elements into a whole.
A type of musical instrument.
A strict separation between cultures.
questions.explanation: "Synthesis" berarti penggabungan berbagai elemen (budaya India dan Jawa) menjadi satu kesatuan baru. Ini menunjukkan bagaimana cerita Wayang Kulit menggabungkan epik India dengan kearifan lokal Jawa. Pilihan (B) adalah definisi yang tepat.

questions.questionText: What role do the Punokawan characters play in Wayang Kulit?
options: They represent the high-ranking Indian gods.
They act as silent observers of the drama.
They offer a local Javanese perspective and social critique.
They are the main villains of the Ramayana.
questions.explanation: Tokoh Punokawan seperti Semar berfungsi sebagai suara rakyat jelata yang memberikan perspektif Jawa dan sering digunakan untuk mengkritik ketidakadilan sosial. Pilihan (C) menjelaskan peran ini.

questions.questionText: How did the Wali Songo use Wayang Kulit during the Islamic transition?
options: They banned the art form for being un-Islamic.
They used it as a method to spread religious teachings.
They changed the puppets into human actors.
They focused only on the entertainment value without any stories.
questions.explanation: Wali Songo memanfaatkan Wayang Kulit sebagai alat dakwah (proselytization) yang efektif untuk menyebarkan ajaran Islam. Pilihan (B) sesuai dengan tujuan mereka.

questions.questionText: The word "proselytization" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
options: Artistic expression.
The act of attempting to convert people to a religion.
Political campaigning.
Economic trade.
questions.explanation: "Proselytization" adalah tindakan mengajak atau mengonversi orang ke dalam suatu agama (dakwah). Ini adalah aktivitas penyebaran agama. Pilihan (B) menjelaskan makna tersebut.

questions.questionText: The author uses the phrase "culturally harmonious rather than disruptive" to imply that:
options: Islam was forced upon the people violently.
The transition to Islam was eased by integrating it with existing traditions.
Wayang Kulit lost its popularity during the Islamic era.
The Hindu-Buddhist stories were completely erased from Indonesian memory.
questions.explanation: Frasa tersebut menyiratkan bahwa proses transisi ke Islam berjalan dengan mulus dan damai karena ajaran baru diintegrasikan secara harmonis dengan tradisi budaya yang sudah ada (Wayang Kulit). Pilihan (B) menangkap makna ini.

questions.questionText: Which of the following can be inferred about the stories of Wayang Kulit?
options: They are identical to the versions found in India.
They have been modified to include local Javanese wisdom and folklore.
They are only understood by the upper class.
They avoid any mention of spirituality or morals.
questions.explanation: Teks secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa cerita-cerita tersebut telah mengalami sintesis budaya, yaitu dengan memasukkan dewa-dewi dan kearifan lokal Jawa. Oleh karena itu, dapat disimpulkan bahwa ceritanya telah dimodifikasi.

questions.questionText: The word "improvise" in paragraph 1 suggests that the dalang:
options: Reads every word from a pre-written script.
Creates parts of the performance spontaneously based on the situation.
Does not know the story before he starts.
Is not allowed to speak during the show.
questions.explanation: "Improvise" berarti menciptakan sesuatu secara spontan atau tanpa naskah yang kaku. Dalang harus mampu berimprovisasi dalam dialog dan diskursus filosofis agar sesuai dengan audiens saat itu. Pilihan (B) adalah deskripsi dari improvisasi.

questions.questionText: What is the primary purpose of the passage?
options: To explain the technical process of making leather puppets.
To argue that Wayang Kulit is the only important Indonesian art form.
To describe Wayang Kulit as a complex cultural and historical synthesis.
To provide a biography of the Wali Songo.
questions.explanation: Tujuan utama teks ini adalah mendeskripsikan dan menganalisis Wayang Kulit sebagai perpaduan (sintesis) budaya yang kompleks—menggabungkan elemen India, Jawa, dan Islam. Pilihan (C) merangkum tujuan keseluruhan tulisan.

passages.title: The Agrarian Heritage of Subak in Bali
passages.content:
In the lush volcanic landscapes of Bali, the Subak system represents a unique intersection of ecology, agriculture, and spirituality that has endured for over a millennium. Subak is a traditional democratic irrigation collective that manages the distribution of water among rice farmers. However, to view Subak merely as a hydraulic feat is to ignore its core philosophy: Tri Hita Karana. This principle emphasizes harmony between three realms: the human, the natural, and the divine. Each Subak collective is centered around a water temple, where rituals are performed to honor Dewi Danu, the goddess of the lakes, ensuring that the management of water is perceived as a sacred duty rather than a mundane administrative task.

The efficacy of the Subak system lies in its sophisticated social organization. Water is diverted from volcanic lakes and rivers through a network of canals and tunnels, eventually reaching the terraced paddies. Decisions regarding planting cycles and water allocation are made communally, ensuring that no single farmer monopolizes the resource at the expense of others. This cooperative approach not only optimizes rice yields but also acts as a natural pest control mechanism; by synchronizing planting across large areas, the Subak prevents the continuous availability of food for pests. Despite the pressures of modern tourism and land development, the Subak remains a vital part of Balinese identity, recognized by UNESCO as a World Heritage site for its cultural and ecological resilience.

questions.questionText: What is the "Subak" system primarily concerned with?
options: The construction of volcanic temples.
The management and distribution of irrigation water.
The promotion of modern tourism in Bali.
The study of volcanic eruptions.
questions.explanation: Sistem Subak adalah sistem kolektif irigasi tradisional yang secara utama bertujuan untuk mengelola dan mendistribusikan air di antara petani padi. Pilihan (B) adalah fokus utama dari sistem ini.

questions.questionText: The word "endured" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Failed
Lasted or persisted
Changed quickly
Disappeared
questions.explanation: "Endured" berarti bertahan atau berlangsung lama (persisted). Ini menunjukkan bahwa sistem Subak telah berhasil bertahan selama lebih dari seribu tahun. Pilihan (B) adalah padanan yang tepat.

questions.questionText: The principle of "Tri Hita Karana" focuses on harmony between:
options: Humans, nature, and the divine.
Farmers, tourists, and government officials.
Water, fire, and air.
Past, present, and future generations.
questions.explanation: Prinsip Tri Hita Karana menekankan keseimbangan atau harmoni antara tiga ranah: manusia (human), alam (natural), dan ilahi/Tuhan (divine). Pilihan (A) mencerminkan fokus utama prinsip ini.

questions.questionText: The word "mundane" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Extraordinary
Ordinary or dull
Holy
Dangerous
questions.explanation: "Mundane" berarti biasa, duniawi, atau membosankan (ordinary). Teks menggunakan kata ini untuk membandingkan tugas administratif yang biasa dengan tugas ritual yang sakral. Pilihan (B) adalah padanan yang paling tepat.

questions.questionText: Why is the Subak system considered "democratic" according to the passage?
options: Because the government dictates all the rules.
Because decisions about water allocation are made communally by farmers.
Because it only allows certain people to vote.
Because it follows international laws.
questions.explanation: Subak dianggap demokratis karena pengambilan keputusan mengenai alokasi air dilakukan secara komunal (bersama) oleh para petani, memastikan tidak ada satu petani pun yang mendominasi. Pilihan (B) menjelaskan mekanisme demokrasi dalam sistem ini.

questions.questionText: The word "efficacy" in paragraph 2 refers to:
options: The cost of a system.
The effectiveness or success of a system.
The age of a system.
The beauty of a system.
questions.explanation: "Efficacy" mengacu pada efektivitas atau kemanjuran suatu sistem. Dalam konteks ini, efficacy merujuk pada seberapa sukses Subak dalam mencapai tujuannya, yaitu mengoptimalkan hasil panen dan mengendalikan hama. Pilihan (B) adalah makna yang tepat.

questions.questionText: How does the Subak system help in pest control?
options: By using advanced chemical pesticides.
By synchronizing planting cycles to limit food for pests.
By importing birds that eat the insects.
By flooding the fields permanently.
questions.explanation: Sistem Subak membantu pengendalian hama dengan cara menyinkronkan waktu penanaman di berbagai area. Ini mencegah ketersediaan makanan yang terus-menerus bagi hama tersebut. Pilihan (B) adalah mekanisme pengendalian hama alami yang dijelaskan dalam teks.

questions.questionText: What can be inferred about the role of water temples in the Subak system?
options: They are purely decorative and have no functional role.
They serve as the physical and spiritual hub for water management.
They are used to store excess rice during the harvest.
They were built recently to attract more tourists.
questions.explanation: Teks menyebutkan bahwa setiap kolektif Subak berpusat di sekitar pura air di mana ritual dilakukan. Ini menyiratkan bahwa pura tersebut berfungsi sebagai pusat fisik (tempat) dan spiritual (ritual) untuk pengelolaan air.

questions.questionText: The word "resilience" in paragraph 2 suggests that the Subak system:
options: Is easily destroyed by modern life.
Has the ability to recover or persist through challenges.
Is no longer relevant in the 21st century.
Requires constant financial aid from UNESCO.
questions.explanation: "Resilience" berarti ketangguhan atau kemampuan untuk bertahan/pulih dari tantangan. Teks menyimpulkan bahwa Subak tetap menjadi bagian penting dari identitas Bali meskipun ada tekanan modern.

questions.questionText: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a threat to the Subak system?
options: Land development.
Modern tourism.
Volcanic eruptions.
These are all mentioned as threats.
questions.explanation: Teks menyebutkan bahwa tekanan yang dihadapi adalah 'modern tourism' dan 'land development'. Letusan gunung berapi (volcanic eruptions) tidak disebutkan sebagai ancaman spesifik terhadap sistem Subak dalam teks ini. Oleh karena itu, (C) adalah jawaban yang benar.

passages.title: The Ethical Implications of Algorithmic Bias
passages.content:
As artificial intelligence (AI) becomes increasingly integrated into the fabric of daily life, from credit scoring to judicial sentencing, the issue of algorithmic bias has emerged as a significant ethical concern. At its core, an algorithm is a set of instructions designed to process data and produce an output. However, if the data used to train these systems contains historical prejudices or reflects societal inequalities, the resulting AI models can inadvertently perpetuate and even amplify these biases. This phenomenon, often termed "garbage in, garbage out," suggests that the perceived objectivity of mathematical models can be deceptive, masking the subjective human biases embedded within the data.

The consequences of algorithmic bias are particularly acute in the realm of predictive policing and hiring. For instance, an AI tool designed to screen job applicants might learn to favor certain demographics based on previous successful hires, effectively excluding qualified candidates from marginalized groups. Similarly, facial recognition technologies have been shown to have significantly higher error rates for individuals with darker skin tones, leading to concerns about wrongful arrests and surveillance. Addressing these issues requires more than just technical fixes; it necessitates a rigorous interdisciplinary approach that involves ethicists, sociologists, and policymakers to ensure that AI systems are transparent, accountable, and equitable.

questions.questionText: What is the primary "ethical concern" discussed in the passage?
options: The high cost of developing AI technology.
The lack of speed in modern computer processors.
The presence of bias in AI algorithms.
The difficulty of learning how to code.
questions.explanation: Kekhawatiran etis utama yang dibahas adalah adanya bias dalam algoritma AI. Paragraf pertama secara eksplisit menyatakan bahwa bias algoritmik adalah masalah etis yang signifikan karena data pelatihan mencerminkan prasangka historis.

questions.questionText: The word "inadvertently" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Intentionally
Unintentionally; by accident
Speedily
Frequently
questions.explanation: "Inadvertently" berarti tanpa disengaja atau secara kebetulan (unintentionally). Ini menunjukkan bahwa bias terjadi bukan karena niat, melainkan karena data yang digunakan.

questions.questionText: What does the phrase "garbage in, garbage out" imply in the context of AI?
options: AI systems are very good at cleaning up literal trash.
If the training data is biased, the resulting AI model will also be biased.
Most AI developers do not care about the environment.
Mathematical models are always 100% objective.
questions.explanation: Frasa "garbage in, garbage out" menyiratkan bahwa kualitas output AI sangat bergantung pada kualitas data input. Jika data (garbage in) mengandung bias atau prasangka, maka model AI yang dihasilkan (garbage out) juga akan mengandung bias tersebut.

questions.questionText: The word "deceptive" in paragraph 1 suggests that mathematical models:
options: Are always the most honest way to represent truth.
Can give a false impression of being fair and objective.
Are too difficult for humans to understand.
Should never be used in science.
questions.explanation: "Deceptive" (menyesatkan) menunjukkan bahwa model matematika, meskipun tampak objektif secara matematis, dapat memberikan kesan palsu tentang keadilan dan objektivitas, padahal mereka mungkin menutupi bias manusia dalam data.

questions.questionText: In the context of hiring, how might an AI tool exhibit bias?
options: By working too fast for human supervisors to keep up.
By favoring candidates from certain demographics based on past data.
By requiring all applicants to have a degree in computer science.
By accidentally deleting all the resumes.
questions.explanation: AI bisa bias dalam proses perekrutan dengan mempelajari pola dari data masa lalu. Jika data tersebut menunjukkan bahwa kelompok demografis tertentu lebih sukses di masa lalu, AI akan cenderung menguntungkan kandidat dari kelompok tersebut.

questions.questionText: The word "acute" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
options: Small or insignificant
Severe or intense
Happy and positive
Scientific
questions.explanation: "Acute" dalam konteks dampak atau masalah berarti sangat serius, parah, atau tajam (severe/intense). Ini menekankan betapa berbahayanya konsekuensi bias algoritmik.

questions.questionText: According to the passage, what is a documented problem with facial recognition technology?
options: It uses too much electricity.
It has higher error rates for people with darker skin tones.
It is unable to recognize people wearing glasses.
It only works in black and white.
questions.explanation: Teks menyebutkan bahwa teknologi pengenalan wajah memiliki tingkat kesalahan yang jauh lebih tinggi untuk individu dengan warna kulit gelap, yang menimbulkan kekhawatiran tentang penangkapan sewenang-wenang.

questions.questionText: What does the author suggest is needed to address algorithmic bias?
options: More powerful computers.
A purely technical solution by engineers.
An interdisciplinary approach involving ethics and policy.
The complete banning of all AI technologies.
questions.explanation: Penulis menyarankan bahwa mengatasi bias algoritmik membutuhkan pendekatan yang lebih luas dan interdisipliner, yaitu melibatkan ahli etika, sosiolog, dan pembuat kebijakan untuk memastikan keadilan.

questions.questionText: The word "equitable" in the final sentence is closest in meaning to:
options: Fair and impartial
Profitable
Fast-moving
Complex
questions.explanation: "Equitable" berarti adil dan tidak memihak (fair). Dalam konteks AI, ini berarti sistem harus memberikan hasil yang setara dan tidak diskriminatif bagi semua kelompok.

questions.questionText: What is the author's attitude toward the 'objectivity' of AI systems?
options: Full confidence in their mathematical perfection.
Skepticism that they can mask human biases.
Indifference toward their social impact.
Hostility toward any form of technology.
questions.explanation: Penulis bersikap skeptis. Penulis berpendapat bahwa objektivitas matematika bisa menipu dan menyembunyikan bias manusia yang tersembunyi dalam data.

passages.title: Batik: The Language of Patterns and Resistance
passages.content:
Batik, the ancient art of wax-resist dyeing, is an integral component of Indonesia’s cultural tapestry and was recognized as a Masterpiece of Oral and Intangible Heritage of Humanity by UNESCO in 2009. While often admired for its aesthetic beauty, Batik serves as a complex visual language where each motif—from the geometric *Parang* to the cloud-like *Mega Mendung*—carries specific symbolic meanings related to social status, nature, and spiritual values. Traditionally, certain patterns were reserved for royalty, such as the *Parang Rusak*, which symbolized the continuous struggle of a king to overcome internal and external obstacles.

Beyond its spiritual and social functions, Batik has also played a role in political identity and resistance. During the Dutch colonial era, the wearing of specific Batik patterns became a subtle form of cultural defiance, asserting an indigenous identity in the face of European hegemony. After independence, President Sukarno actively promoted Batik as a national dress to foster a unified Indonesian identity across the diverse archipelago. Today, the Batik industry faces the challenge of balancing traditional hand-drawn techniques (*Batik Tulis*) with modern mass-production methods (*Batik Cap*). While mass production makes Batik more accessible, the preservation of the slow, meditative process of *Batik Tulis* remains essential for maintaining the art form's deep cultural and spiritual integrity.

questions.questionText: The word "integral" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Unnecessary
Essential; fundamental
Very expensive
Decorative
questions.explanation: "Integral" berarti sangat penting, mendasar, atau esensial. Batik dianggap sebagai komponen yang tidak terpisahkan dari permadani budaya Indonesia.

questions.questionText: What does the *Parang Rusak* motif traditionally symbolize?
options: The beauty of the clouds during a storm.
A king's struggle against obstacles.
The end of the rice harvest season.
The peacefulness of the ocean.
questions.explanation: Motif *Parang Rusak* secara tradisional melambangkan perjuangan yang berkelanjutan seorang raja untuk mengatasi rintangan internal maupun eksternal.

questions.questionText: The word "defiance" in paragraph 2 refers to:
options: Complete obedience.
Bold resistance or opposition.
Military cooperation.
Artistic inspiration.
questions.explanation: "Defiance" berarti perlawanan atau penentangan. Dalam konteks kolonial, penggunaan motif Batik adalah bentuk perlawanan budaya yang halus terhadap dominasi Eropa.

questions.questionText: How was Batik used during the Dutch colonial era according to the passage?
options: It was exported to Europe as the main source of income.
It was worn as a subtle way to assert indigenous identity and resist hegemony.
It was used to create uniforms for the Dutch army.
It was banned by the colonial government.
questions.explanation: Selama era kolonial Belanda, mengenakan pola Batik tertentu menjadi bentuk perlawanan budaya yang halus untuk menegaskan identitas pribumi di hadapan hegemoni Eropa.

questions.questionText: Why did President Sukarno promote Batik after independence?
options: To help increase the price of cotton.
To foster a unified national identity.
To compete with the fashion industries of neighboring countries.
To replace all other forms of traditional art.
questions.explanation: Presiden Sukarno mempromosikan Batik sebagai pakaian nasional untuk menumbuhkan dan menyatukan identitas Indonesia yang beragam di seluruh nusantara.

questions.questionText: What is the main difference between *Batik Tulis* and *Batik Cap*?
options: Batik Tulis is made by machines, while Batik Cap is made by hand.
Batik Tulis uses wax, while Batik Cap uses digital printing.
Batik Tulis is a hand-drawn traditional technique, while Batik Cap is a mass-production method.
There is no difference mentioned in the text.
questions.explanation: Perbedaan utamanya adalah *Batik Tulis* adalah teknik tradisional yang dibuat dengan tangan (slow, meditative process), sedangkan *Batik Cap* adalah metode produksi massal.

questions.questionText: The word "integrity" in the final sentence is closest in meaning to:
options: Profitability
Soundness or moral consistency
Complexity
Speed
questions.explanation: "Integrity" merujuk pada keutuhan atau konsistensi nilai (moral dan budaya). Ini menekankan pentingnya menjaga kualitas spiritual dan budaya dari seni Batik.

questions.questionText: The word "accessible" in paragraph 2 suggests that mass-produced Batik is:
options: Harder to find in stores.
Available to and affordable for more people.
Higher in spiritual value than hand-drawn Batik.
Only sold in international markets.
questions.explanation: "Accessible" berarti mudah didapat atau dijangkau oleh lebih banyak orang. Ini mengacu pada fakta bahwa produksi massal membuat Batik lebih terjangkau.

questions.questionText: What does the passage imply about the future of Batik?
options: Traditional techniques will likely disappear completely.
Modern methods are necessary but traditional methods must be preserved to keep its cultural essence.
UNESCO will soon remove Batik from its list.
Batik will eventually be replaced by Western clothing.
questions.explanation: Implikasi dari teks adalah bahwa meskipun metode produksi massal diperlukan untuk aksesibilitas, pelestarian proses tradisional (*Batik Tulis*) sangat penting untuk menjaga integritas budaya dan spiritualnya.

questions.questionText: Which of the following is a motif mentioned in the passage?
options: Srikandi
Mega Mendung
Borobudur
Gamelan
questions.explanation: *Mega Mendung* disebutkan dalam teks sebagai salah satu contoh motif Batik yang memiliki makna simbolis.

passages.title: The Resource Curse and Economic Diversification
passages.content:
The "resource curse," also known as the Dutch Disease, is a paradoxical economic phenomenon where countries with an abundance of natural resources—such as oil, gas, or minerals—tend to experience less economic growth and worse development outcomes than countries with fewer natural resources. This occurs because a sudden influx of foreign currency from resource exports causes the nation's exchange rate to appreciate, making other sectors, particularly manufacturing and agriculture, less competitive on the global market. Consequently, the economy becomes dangerously over-reliant on a single, volatile commodity.

To mitigate the effects of the resource curse, economists advocate for aggressive economic diversification and the establishment of Sovereign Wealth Funds (SWFs). Diversification involves investing in human capital and infrastructure to foster a robust private sector outside of the extractive industries. Meanwhile, SWFs act as a fiscal buffer, sequestering a portion of resource revenues to be invested in international assets. This not only prevents the local currency from over-appreciating but also ensures that the wealth generated from finite resources is preserved for future generations. Countries like Norway are often cited as successful models for navigating this complex economic landscape through disciplined fiscal policy and transparent governance.

questions.questionText: What is the primary paradox of the "resource curse"?
options: Resources are more expensive in countries that produce them.
Abundant natural resources can lead to slower economic growth.
Foreign currency is actually worth less than local currency.
Manufacturing sectors thrive when oil prices are high.
questions.explanation: Paradoksnya adalah negara dengan sumber daya alam melimpah justru cenderung mengalami pertumbuhan ekonomi yang lebih lambat dan hasil pembangunan yang lebih buruk dibandingkan negara dengan sumber daya yang lebih sedikit.

questions.questionText: The word "influx" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Departure
Arrival or inward flow
Decrease
Stability
questions.explanation: "Influx" berarti aliran masuk dalam jumlah besar (inward flow). Dalam konteks ini, itu merujuk pada masuknya mata uang asing dari ekspor sumber daya.

questions.questionText: How does a strong exchange rate affect a country's manufacturing sector?
options: It makes manufactured goods cheaper for foreign buyers.
It makes manufactured goods more expensive and less competitive internationally.
It encourages more people to work in factories.
It has no measurable effect on trade.
questions.explanation: Nilai tukar yang menguat membuat barang manufaktur menjadi lebih mahal bagi pembeli asing, sehingga mengurangi daya saing mereka di pasar global.

questions.questionText: The word "volatile" in paragraph 1 suggests that commodity prices:
options: Are always very high.
Change quickly and unpredictably.
Are set by the government.
Never change over time.
questions.explanation: "Volatile" berarti tidak stabil atau berubah-ubah secara cepat dan tidak terduga. Hal ini menggambarkan risiko yang dihadapi ekonomi yang terlalu bergantung pada komoditas tunggal.

questions.questionText: What is one primary function of a Sovereign Wealth Fund (SWF)?
options: To spend all resource revenue immediately on social programs.
To act as a fiscal buffer by investing revenue in international assets.
To print more local currency to lower the exchange rate.
To replace the private sector entirely.
questions.explanation: Fungsi utama SWF adalah bertindak sebagai penyangga fiskal dengan mengisolasi sebagian pendapatan sumber daya untuk diinvestasikan ke aset internasional, sehingga mencegah apresiasi mata uang lokal.

questions.questionText: The word "sequestering" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
options: Spending
Setting aside or isolating
Borrowing
Discovering
questions.explanation: "Sequestering" berarti menyisihkan atau mengisolasi (dana). Dalam konteks ini, dana sumber daya diisolasi untuk investasi di luar negeri.

questions.questionText: According to the passage, economic diversification requires investment in:
options: More oil drilling equipment.
Human capital and infrastructure.
Foreign luxury goods.
Only the mining sector.
questions.explanation: Diversifikasi ekonomi memerlukan investasi pada modal manusia (pendidikan dan keterampilan) serta infrastruktur untuk membangun sektor swasta yang kuat di luar industri ekstraktif.

questions.questionText: Why is Norway mentioned at the end of the passage?
options: Because it has the most natural resources in the world.
As an example of a country that successfully managed its resource wealth.
To show that even developed countries suffer from the curse.
Because it is the only country with a manufacturing sector.
questions.explanation: Norwegia dijadikan contoh negara yang berhasil menavigasi lanskap ekonomi yang kompleks melalui kebijakan fiskal yang disiplin dan tata kelola yang transparan.

questions.questionText: The word "finite" in paragraph 2 means:
options: Unlimited
Limited; having an end
Expensive
Liquid
questions.explanation: "Finite" berarti terbatas atau memiliki akhir (tidak terbarukan). Ini merujuk pada sifat sumber daya alam yang tidak dapat diulang secara terus-menerus.

questions.questionText: Which of the following best summarizes the main idea of the passage?
options: Natural resources are the only way for a nation to become wealthy.
High exchange rates are always beneficial for a nation's exports.
Countries must use disciplined fiscal policies to avoid the negative economic impacts of resource abundance.
Sovereign Wealth Funds are too risky for most developing nations.
questions.explanation: Gagasan utama paragraf ini adalah bahwa untuk menghindari 'kutukan sumber daya', negara-negara harus menerapkan kebijakan fiskal yang disiplin dan melakukan diversifikasi ekonomi.

passages.title: Neuroplasticity and the Aging Brain
passages.content:
For decades, the prevailing scientific dogma held that the adult brain was a static organ, incapable of generating new neurons or significantly altering its structure after a certain age. However, the emergence of the concept of neuroplasticity has revolutionized our understanding of cerebral function. Neuroplasticity refers to the brain's ability to reorganize itself by forming new neural connections throughout life. This process allows the neurons in the brain to compensate for injury and disease and to adjust their activities in response to new situations or changes in their environment.

Research has shown that neuroplasticity is not limited to childhood but persists into old age, albeit at a slightly reduced rate. Engaging in cognitively demanding activities, such as learning a new language or playing a musical instrument, has been found to stimulate synaptogenesis—the formation of new synapses between neurons. Furthermore, physical exercise has been linked to increased levels of brain-derived neurotrophic factor (BDNF), a protein that supports the survival of existing neurons and encourages the growth of new ones. These findings suggest that the brain's health is highly dependent on a lifestyle that prioritizes mental stimulation and physical activity, challenging the notion that cognitive decline is an inevitable part of aging.

questions.questionText: What was the "prevailing scientific dogma" regarding the adult brain?
options: It was constantly growing and changing.
It was a static organ incapable of major structural changes.
It only functioned during sleep.
It could only be repaired through surgery.
questions.explanation: Dogma sains sebelumnya menganggap otak dewasa bersifat statis dan tidak mampu menghasilkan neuron baru atau mengubah strukturnya secara signifikan setelah mencapai usia tertentu.

questions.questionText: The word "emergence" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Disappearance
Rise or appearance
Confusion
Limitation
questions.explanation: "Emergence" berarti kemunculan atau timbulnya (rise/appearance). Ini merujuk pada munculnya konsep neuroplastisitas yang merevolusi pemahaman kita tentang fungsi otak.

questions.questionText: How does neuroplasticity help the brain according to the passage?
options: By making the brain larger in physical size.
By allowing it to reorganize and form new connections.
By preventing the brain from needing oxygen.
By stopping all neural activity during an injury.
questions.explanation: Neuroplastisitas membantu otak dengan memungkinkan otak untuk mengatur ulang dirinya sendiri dan membentuk koneksi saraf (neural connections) yang baru sepanjang hidup.

questions.questionText: The word "albeit" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
options: Therefore
Although
Because
Especially
questions.explanation: "Albeit" berarti meskipun atau walaupun (although). Kata ini digunakan untuk menunjukkan pengecualian atau kontras, yaitu neuroplastisitas terjadi, *walaupun* lajunya sedikit berkurang di usia tua.

questions.questionText: What is "synaptogenesis"?
options: The loss of memory in old age.
The formation of new connections between neurons.
A type of brain disease.
The process of the brain shrinking.
questions.explanation: Sinaptogenesis adalah proses pembentukan sinapsis, yaitu koneksi baru yang terbentuk antara neuron-neuron di otak, yang dipicu oleh aktivitas kognitif atau olahraga.

questions.questionText: According to the passage, how does physical exercise affect the brain?
options: It reduces the amount of blood flow to the head.
It increases levels of BDNF, which supports neuron growth.
It makes the brain use more sugar than necessary.
It has no impact on brain health.
questions.explanation: Latihan fisik dikaitkan dengan peningkatan kadar BDNF (protein neurotrofik yang diturunkan dari otak), yang mendukung kelangsungan hidup neuron yang sudah ada dan mendorong pertumbuhan neuron baru.

questions.questionText: The word "persists" in paragraph 2 means:
options: Stops immediately
Continues to exist
Becomes dangerous
Fades away
questions.explanation: "Persists" berarti terus berlanjut atau tetap ada (continues to exist). Ini menjelaskan bahwa neuroplastisitas tidak hilang seiring bertambahnya usia.

questions.questionText: The author implies that cognitive decline:
options: Is strictly determined by genetics.
Is completely unavoidable for everyone.
Can potentially be mitigated through lifestyle choices.
Is a myth and does not actually happen.
questions.explanation: Penulis mengimplikasikan bahwa penurunan kognitif tidaklah tak terhindarkan; kesehatan otak sangat bergantung pada gaya hidup yang mendukung stimulasi mental dan aktivitas fisik.

questions.questionText: The word "inevitable" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
options: Unavoidable
Rare
Pleasant
Optional
questions.explanation: "Inevitable" berarti tidak dapat dihindari (unavoidable). Ini adalah lawan dari gagasan bahwa penurunan kognitif adalah sesuatu yang pasti terjadi.

questions.questionText: Which of the following would be the most suitable title for this passage?
options: The Dangers of Modern Neuroscience
Neuroplasticity: The Adaptable Brain Throughout Life
Why Children Learn Faster Than Adults
The Role of Proteins in Muscle Growth
questions.explanation: Judul yang paling tepat adalah 'Neuroplasticity: The Adaptable Brain Throughout Life' karena fokus utama teks adalah pada kemampuan otak untuk reorganisasi dan adaptasi sepanjang hidup.

passages.title: Exoplanets and the Search for Biosignatures
passages.content:
The discovery of thousands of exoplanets—planets orbiting stars outside our solar system—has transformed the field of astrobiology. Scientists are no longer merely cataloging these worlds; they are now searching for "biosignatures," which are substances or phenomena that provide scientific evidence of past or present life. The most promising candidates are planets located in the "Habitable Zone," often called the Goldilocks Zone, where conditions are neither too hot nor too cold for liquid water to exist on the surface. However, the presence of water is only the first step; researchers must also analyze the chemical composition of an exoplanet's atmosphere.

Using spectroscopy, astronomers can detect the distinct chemical fingerprints of gases like oxygen, methane, and carbon dioxide. While oxygen is a strong indicator of life on Earth, its presence alone is not definitive proof of extraterrestrial biology, as it can also be produced by non-biological processes such as the photodissociation of water vapor. A more compelling biosignature would be the simultaneous detection of oxygen and methane in a state of chemical disequilibrium. On Earth, these two gases react and destroy each other quickly; their coexistence in an atmosphere would imply that they are being constantly replenished by living organisms. As telescopes like the James Webb Space Telescope (JWST) begin to probe these distant atmospheres, the possibility of detecting life beyond Earth moves from the realm of science fiction to empirical science.

questions.questionText: What are "biosignatures" as defined in the passage?
options: Signals sent by advanced civilizations.
Substances or phenomena that provide evidence of life.
The names given to newly discovered stars.
Special telescopes used to find exoplanets.
questions.explanation: Biosignatures didefinisikan sebagai zat atau fenomena yang memberikan bukti ilmiah adanya kehidupan (past or present life) di suatu planet.

questions.questionText: The "Goldilocks Zone" refers to an area where:
options: Liquid water can potentially exist on a planet's surface.
The gravity is exactly the same as Earth's.
Only giant gas planets can be found.
Stars are at the end of their life cycles.
questions.explanation: Zona Goldilocks (Habitable Zone) adalah wilayah di mana suhu memungkinkan adanya air cair di permukaan planet, sehingga menjadi kandidat yang paling menjanjikan untuk kehidupan.

questions.questionText: The word "cataloging" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Destroying
Systematic listing or recording
Visiting in person
Creating through chemistry
questions.explanation: "Cataloging" berarti mendaftarkan atau mencatat secara sistematis. Para ilmuwan tidak hanya mengumpulkan data, tetapi juga mendaftarkannya secara terstruktur.

questions.questionText: How does spectroscopy help astronomers?
options: It allows them to travel to exoplanets faster.
It detects chemical fingerprints in distant atmospheres.
It measures the exact weight of a star.
It creates oxygen in lab environments.
questions.explanation: Spektroskopi digunakan oleh astronom untuk mendeteksi jejak kimiawi yang khas (chemical fingerprints) dari gas-gas tertentu di atmosfer planet yang jauh.

questions.questionText: Why is oxygen not considered "definitive proof" of life?
options: Because it is a poisonous gas for most organisms.
Because it can be produced by non-biological processes.
Because it is impossible to detect with telescopes.
Because Earth's atmosphere has no oxygen.
questions.explanation: Oksigen bukan bukti definitif karena oksigen dapat diproduksi oleh proses non-biologis, seperti fotodissosiasi uap air, sehingga keberadaannya sendiri tidak menjamin adanya kehidupan.

questions.questionText: The word "compelling" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
options: Boring
Persuasive or convincing
Dangerous
Hidden
questions.explanation: "Compelling" berarti meyakinkan atau kuat (persuasive). Ini digunakan untuk menjelaskan mengapa deteksi oksigen dan metana secara bersamaan merupakan biosignatur yang lebih kuat.

questions.questionText: What does the presence of both oxygen and methane signify?
options: That the planet is about to explode.
A state of chemical equilibrium.
Potential replenishment by living organisms.
The complete absence of any atmosphere.
questions.explanation: Kehadiran oksigen dan metana secara bersamaan (dalam keadaan ketidakseimbangan kimia) mengimplikasikan bahwa gas-gas tersebut sedang diisi kembali oleh organisme hidup.

questions.questionText: The word "replenished" in paragraph 2 means:
options: Used up completely
Refilled or restored
Frozen into ice
Changed into a solid
questions.explanation: "Replenished" berarti diisi kembali atau dipulihkan. Ini menjelaskan bahwa koeksistensi O2 dan metana menyiratkan pengisian ulang yang berkelanjutan oleh organisme hidup.

questions.questionText: The word "empirical" in the final sentence suggests that the study is based on:
options: Imagination
Observation and evidence
Religious beliefs
Popular opinion
questions.explanation: "Empirical" berarti berdasarkan observasi, eksperimen, dan bukti nyata. Ini menunjukkan bahwa kemungkinan menemukan kehidupan di luar Bumi didasarkan pada data ilmiah.

questions.questionText: According to the passage, the JWST will be used to:
options: Land robots on the surface of exoplanets.
Probe and analyze distant atmospheres for biosignatures.
Move Earth closer to the habital zone.
Destroy dangerous asteroids.
questions.explanation: Teleskop James Webb (JWST) digunakan untuk menyelidiki dan menganalisis atmosfer planet-planet jauh guna mencari tanda-tanda kehidupan (biosignatures).

passages.title: The Heat Island Effect and Urban Resilience
passages.content:
As urbanization accelerates, cities across the globe are grappling with the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect, a phenomenon where metropolitan areas experience significantly higher temperatures than their surrounding rural counterparts. This temperature disparity is primarily caused by the prevalence of heat-absorbing surfaces, such as asphalt and concrete, and a lack of vegetation, which reduces the cooling effects of evapotranspiration. Additionally, waste heat generated by vehicles, air conditioning units, and industrial processes contributes to the elevated thermal profile of urban centers. The consequences are not merely uncomfortable; they are a matter of public health, as extreme heat can exacerbate respiratory and cardiovascular conditions.

To build urban resilience against the UHI effect, city planners are increasingly adopting "green infrastructure" strategies. One effective method is the implementation of green roofs—rooftops covered with vegetation—which provide insulation and reduce the amount of heat absorbed by buildings. Another approach involves the use of "cool pavements," which are designed with high solar reflectance to bounce sunlight back into the atmosphere. Furthermore, expanding urban tree canopies provides essential shade and enhances natural cooling. These interventions not only lower local temperatures but also reduce energy consumption for cooling, creating a more sustainable and livable urban environment in the face of a changing climate.

questions.questionText: What is the Urban Heat Island (UHI) effect?
options: Cities becoming islands due to rising sea levels.
Urban areas being warmer than surrounding rural areas.
A type of tropical weather found only on islands.
The total absence of heat in city centers.
questions.explanation: Efek Urban Heat Island (UHI) adalah fenomena di mana wilayah metropolitan mengalami suhu yang jauh lebih tinggi dibandingkan dengan daerah pedesaan di sekitarnya. Pilihan lain tidak mendefinisikan efek UHI.

questions.questionText: The word "disparity" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Similarity
Difference or inequality
Stability
Measurement
questions.explanation: "Disparity" berarti perbedaan atau ketimpangan. Dalam konteks ini, merujuk pada perbedaan suhu yang signifikan antara kota dan daerah pedesaan.

questions.questionText: Which of the following is NOT mentioned as a cause of the UHI effect?
options: Asphalt surfaces
Waste heat from air conditioners
Lack of agricultural fertilizer
Reduced vegetation
questions.explanation: Penyebab UHI yang disebutkan meliputi permukaan menyerap panas (aspal dan beton), kurangnya vegetasi, dan panas sisa dari kendaraan serta AC. Kekurangan pupuk pertanian tidak disebutkan sebagai penyebab.

questions.questionText: The word "exacerbate" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Improve
Make worse or aggravate
Cure
Ignore
questions.explanation: "Exacerbate" berarti memperburuk atau memperparah. Dalam konteks teks, panas ekstrem memperburuk kondisi kesehatan.

questions.questionText: How do green roofs help reduce heat in buildings?
options: By reflecting all light with mirrors.
By providing insulation and reducing heat absorption.
By using more electricity for fans.
By turning into solar panels.
questions.explanation: Atap hijau membantu mengurangi panas dengan menyediakan isolasi dan meminimalkan jumlah panas yang diserap oleh bangunan.

questions.questionText: The word "resilience" in paragraph 2 refers to the ability to:
options: Withstand or recover from challenges.
Move to a new location.
Increase temperatures further.
Build taller skyscrapers.
questions.explanation: "Resilience" (ketangguhan) merujuk pada kemampuan untuk bertahan atau pulih dari tantangan lingkungan seperti efek UHI.

questions.questionText: What is the primary feature of "cool pavements"?
options: They are made of solid ice.
They have high solar reflectance.
They are always painted dark blue.
They can only be used in rural areas.
questions.explanation: Fitur utama dari cool pavements adalah memiliki reflektansi surya yang tinggi agar memantulkan sinar matahari kembali ke atmosfer.

questions.questionText: The term "tree canopies" refers to:
options: The roots of a tree underground.
The upper layer of tree leaves and branches.
The fruit produced by urban trees.
The wood used for building houses.
questions.explanation: Tree canopies merujuk pada lapisan atas dari daun dan dahan pohon yang berfungsi memberikan naungan alami.

questions.questionText: The word "interventions" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
options: Inventions
Actions or measures taken to improve a situation
Arguments
Interruptions
questions.explanation: "Interventions" dalam konteks ini berarti tindakan atau langkah strategis yang diambil oleh perencana kota untuk mengatasi efek UHI.

questions.questionText: What secondary benefit of UHI mitigation is mentioned in the passage?
options: Increased vehicle speed.
Lower energy consumption for cooling.
Higher taxes for city residents.
A reduction in the number of urban trees.
questions.explanation: Manfaat sekunder yang disebutkan adalah bahwa intervensi ini tidak hanya menurunkan suhu tetapi juga mengurangi konsumsi energi untuk pendinginan ruangan.

passages.title: The Bystander Effect and Social Influence
passages.content:
The bystander effect is a social psychological phenomenon in which individuals are less likely to offer help to a victim when other people are present. First systematically studied by Bibb Latané and John Darley following the 1964 murder of Kitty Genovese, the theory suggests that the presence of others creates a "diffusion of responsibility." In this state, each individual feels that someone else will intervene, leading to a collective paralysis. Furthermore, the phenomenon is reinforced by "pluralistic ignorance," where bystanders look to others to gauge the seriousness of the situation. If no one else is reacting, an individual may conclude that help is not required, even if the situation is actually an emergency.

To overcome the bystander effect, psychologists emphasize the importance of individual awareness and specific action. In emergency training, individuals are often taught to single out one person from a crowd and give them a direct instruction, such as "You in the red shirt, call 911." This eliminates the diffusion of responsibility by placing the burden of action on a specific individual. Understanding these social dynamics is crucial for fostering more prosocial behavior in public spaces and ensuring that emergencies are met with timely intervention rather than passive observation.

questions.questionText: What is the core concept of the bystander effect?
options: People help more when in large groups.
People are less likely to help when others are present.
Bystanders are usually the cause of the emergency.
Victims should never ask for help from strangers.
questions.explanation: Konsep inti dari efek bystander adalah bahwa individu cenderung kurang bersedia menawarkan bantuan kepada korban ketika ada orang lain yang hadir di sekitar mereka.

questions.questionText: What is "diffusion of responsibility"?
options: When everyone takes equal charge of a situation.
The feeling that someone else will take action, so you don't have to.
The process of spreading news to the media.
A legal requirement to help everyone.
questions.explanation: "Diffusion of responsibility" adalah perasaan bahwa karena banyak orang hadir, tanggung jawab untuk bertindak tersebar ke semua orang, sehingga setiap individu merasa tidak perlu mengambil tindakan.

questions.questionText: The word "paralysis" in paragraph 1 suggests:
options: A physical inability to walk.
A state of inaction or inability to decide.
Extreme speed and efficiency.
A loud noise made by a crowd.
questions.explanation: "Paralysis" dalam konteks sosial merujuk pada keadaan tidak bertindak atau kelumpuhan kolektif karena tidak ada yang mau mengambil inisiatif.

questions.questionText: What is "pluralistic ignorance"?
options: Knowing too much about a subject.
Looking at others' lack of reaction to judge an emergency as not serious.
Refusing to learn new information.
The ability to speak multiple languages poorly.
questions.explanation: Pluralistic ignorance adalah kondisi di mana seseorang melihat orang lain tidak bereaksi, lalu menyimpulkan bahwa situasi tersebut tidak serius, padahal mungkin merupakan keadaan darurat.

questions.questionText: The word "gauge" in paragraph 1 is closest in meaning to:
options: Ignore
Estimate or assess
Increase
Forget
questions.explanation: "Gauge" berarti memperkirakan atau menilai (assess), digunakan di sini untuk menjelaskan bagaimana orang menggunakan reaksi orang lain untuk mengukur tingkat keparahan situasi.

questions.questionText: How can the diffusion of responsibility be eliminated in an emergency?
options: By shouting as loud as possible to the whole crowd.
By giving a direct instruction to a specific individual.
By waiting for a police officer to arrive.
By leaving the scene to find help elsewhere.
questions.explanation: Untuk menghilangkan difusi tanggung jawab, tindakan yang disarankan adalah memberikan instruksi langsung kepada satu orang spesifik untuk bertindak.

questions.questionText: The word "burden" in paragraph 2 is closest in meaning to:
options: Gift
Responsibility or weight
Song
Speed
questions.explanation: "Burden" berarti beban atau tanggung jawab (responsibility), merujuk pada kewajiban untuk bertindak dalam situasi darurat.

questions.questionText: The word "prosocial" in the final sentence refers to behavior that:
options: Benefits society and other people.
Is only done for professional gain.
Is anti-social and aggressive.
Involves using social media.
questions.explanation: "Prosocial" merujuk pada perilaku yang menguntungkan atau bermanfaat bagi orang lain dan masyarakat secara keseluruhan.

questions.questionText: The author implies that the bystander effect is caused by:
